blocks:
Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ ٱتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ وَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ وَٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا (۱)
O Prophet! Be wary of Allah and do not obey the faithless and the hypocrites. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (1)

Key Points

The word «ایّها» (Ayyuhā) following the vocative particle «یا» (Yā) is intended so that others, besides the direct addressee, also pay attention. When it is said: «یا رسول اللّه» (O Messenger of Allah), the addressee is only the Prophet, but «یا ایّها الرّسول» (O you Messenger) means: O people! I am calling the Prophet. (366)

366) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr Razi.

Messages

1- The Prophet of Islam holds a special status in the presence of Allah. «یا ایّها النّبىّ» (In the Qur'an, other prophets are called by name: Yā Ibrāhīm! Yā Nūḥ! Yā Mūsā! Yā Yaḥyā! But «Yā Muḥammad» has not appeared in the Qur'an.)

2- Even the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is in need of divine admonition. «یا ایّها النّبى اتّق اللّه»

3- The command directed to the Prophet is, in reality, a command directed to his followers. «یا ایّها النّبى»

4- By reviving good (Ma'rūf), we prevent evil (Munkar). (First, obedience to Allah, then distance from the disbelievers and the hypocrites.) «اتّق اللّه و لاتطع الكافرین و المنافقین»

5- Following the disbelievers and the hypocrites is a lack of piety (Taqwā). «اتّق اللّه و لاتطع الكافرین و المنافقین»

6- The disbelievers and the hypocrites intend to attract the attention of religious leaders to themselves by any means possible. «لاتطع الكافرین و المنافقین»

7- One can only combat deviations and compromising proposals when faith (Īmān) and piety (Taqwā) are strong. «اتّق اللّه ولا تطع»

8- The disbelievers and the hypocrites are on the same path. «الكافرین والمنافقین»

9- Obedience to the All-Knowing (Alīm) and All-Wise (Ḥakīm) God is justified, «علیماً حكیماً», but what justification is there for following the disbelievers and hypocrites who lack knowledge and wisdom? «اتّق اللّه... علیماً حكیما»

10- The source of Allah's commands regarding the abandonment of obedience to the disbelievers and hypocrites is His knowledge and wisdom. «علیماً حكیماً»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Ahzab

This Surah was revealed in Medina and has seventy-three verses. The Jews of Medina, the polytheists of Mecca, and the hypocrites hidden among the Muslims united in the fifth year of the Hijra to wage war against the Muslims. The Prophet and his companions dug a trench (khandaq) around Medina in response to their attack. This war became known as the Battle of Al-Ahzab (The Confederates) because it was planned by various groups, and it became known as the Battle of the Trench (Khandaq) because the Muslims dug a trench to defend themselves. In total, seventeen verses of this Surah concern the Battle of Al-Ahzab, and because the word Ahzab (Confederates) is used three times in verses 20 and 22, the Surah was named "Al-Ahzab." In addition to the Battle of Al-Ahzab, issues concerning Zihār (a form of pre-Islamic divorce) and Jahiliyyah divorce, the rulings on adoption, the subject of Hijab, and attention to the Resurrection (Ma'ād) are discussed in this Surah.

Ayah

وَٱتَّبِعْ مَا يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرًا (۲)
Follow that which is revealed to you from your Lord. Indeed Allah is well aware of what you do. (2)

Messages

1- Whenever you close a path of deviation, open a clear path. «لا تطع - واتّبع»

2- The path to achieving piety (taqwa) is solely following the revelation. (In the previous Ayah He said: «اتّق اللّه», in this Ayah He says: «اتّبع ما يوحى إليك»)

3- Revelation is among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah). «من ربّك» (Following revelation is the means of your growth and training.) «ربّك»

4- Belief in Divine knowledge is the strongest leverage point for following the revelation. «واتّبع ... انّ اللّه... خبير»

Ayahs

وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ وَكِيلًا (۳)
And put your trust in Allah; Allah suffices as trustee. (3)
مَّا جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ لِرَجُلٍ مِّن قَلْبَيْنِ فِى جَوْفِهِۦ ۚ وَمَا جَعَلَ أَزْوَٰجَكُمُ ٱلَّـٰٓـِٔى تُظَـٰهِرُونَ مِنْهُنَّ أُمَّهَـٰتِكُمْ ۚ وَمَا جَعَلَ أَدْعِيَآءَكُمْ أَبْنَآءَكُمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ قَوْلُكُم بِأَفْوَٰهِكُمْ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ يَقُولُ ٱلْحَقَّ وَهُوَ يَهْدِى ٱلسَّبِيلَ (٤)
Allah has not put two hearts within any man, nor has He made your wives whom you repudiate by zihar your mothers, nor has he made your adopted sons your [actual] sons. These are mere utterances of your mouths. But Allah speaks the truth and He guides to the [right] way. (4)

Key Points

Ad'iyā' (ادعیاء) is the plural of da'iyy (دَعىّ), meaning the claim or attribution of a child or something else to others. Ẓihār (ظِهار) means when a man tells his wife: "You are forbidden to me like my mother." This act, which was customary during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) as a form of divorce, is forbidden (harām) and requires expiation (kaffārah). For further explanation, refer to books of jurisprudence (fiqh). The Quran attributes inner feelings, emotions, and inclinations to the human heart (qalb).

Messages

1- Not following the disbelievers (kuffār) and hypocrites (munāfiqūn) and instead following revelation (waḥy) involves difficulties, the way to overcome which is reliance upon Allah (tawakkul). «لا تطع - واتّبع - و توكّل»

2- We should strive to state the reason for the commands. «توكّل على اللّه - كفى باللّه وكیلا» (What does he lack who has Allah?)

3- Man's innate and heartfelt inclinations are only one thing, and whatever a person says or does contrary to that is his personal hypocrisy, not the Divine Will. «و ما جَعَل اللّه لرجل من قلبین»

4- Two contradictory loves cannot be gathered in one heart. «من قلبین» (Love and following revelation are incompatible with the guardianship and following of the disbelievers and hypocrites.)

5- The relationship between parents and child is a real and natural relationship, not a ceremonial or contractual one. (Neither does a wife become like a mother, «ما جعل ازواجكم الّآئى تظاهرون منهنّ امّهاتكم» nor does an adopted child become a son. «ما جعل ادعیائكم ابنائكم»)

6- One of the duties of the prophets is to remove superstitions from society. «ما جعل... ذلكم قولكم بافواهكم»

7- The criterion for truth (ḥaqq) and falsehood (bāṭil) is Divine revelation, not social customs, traditions, or habits. «ذلكم قولكم بافواهكم و اللّه یقول الحقّ»

Ayah

ٱدْعُوهُمْ لِـَٔابَآئِهِمْ هُوَ أَقْسَطُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ فَإِن لَّمْ تَعْلَمُوٓا۟ ءَابَآءَهُمْ فَإِخْوَٰنُكُمْ فِى ٱلدِّينِ وَمَوَٰلِيكُمْ ۚ وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَآ أَخْطَأْتُم بِهِۦ وَلَـٰكِن مَّا تَعَمَّدَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (٥)
Call them after their fathers. That is more just with Allah. If you do not know their fathers, then they are your brethren in the faith and your kinsmen. Excepting what your hearts may intend deliberately, there will be no sin upon you for any mistake that you may make therein. And Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (5)

Messages

1- We must preserve the lineage relationship of individuals with their real fathers. «ادعوهم لابائهم» (The name of the father must be included in the identity documents of individuals.)

2- We must treat individuals whose fathers are unknown respectfully, affectionately, and fraternally. «فاخوانكم فى الدین» (These were individuals who were taken captive in wars and subsequently became Muslims, but the Muslims did not know their fathers.)

3- "Religious brother" (برادر دینى) is a Quranic term. «اخوانكم فى الدین»

4- Call the freed slaves whose fathers are unknown "friends" and "protectors/allies" (mawla). «و موالیكم»

5- Intention, purpose, and awareness are effective factors in committing a crime. «اخطأتم - تعمّدت قلوبكم»

6- The conscious alteration of identity documents and lineages is a crime. «و لكن ما تعمّدت قلوبكم»

7- The disregard of unintentional and erroneous sins stems from the Forgiveness and Mercy of Allah. «لیس علیكم جناح فیما اخطأتم... و كان اللّه غفوراً رحیماً»

8- Allah's forgiveness is accompanied by kindness and compassion. «غفوراً رحیماً»

Ayah

ٱلنَّبِىُّ أَوْلَىٰ بِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ ۖ وَأَزْوَٰجُهُۥٓ أُمَّهَـٰتُهُمْ ۗ وَأُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَرْحَامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلَىٰ بِبَعْضٍ فِى كِتَـٰبِ ٱللَّهِ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُهَـٰجِرِينَ إِلَّآ أَن تَفْعَلُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَوْلِيَآئِكُم مَّعْرُوفًا ۚ كَانَ ذَٰلِكَ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مَسْطُورًا (٦)
The Prophet is closer to the faithful than their own souls, and his wives are their mothers. The blood relatives are more entitled to inherit from one another in the Book of Allah than the [other] faithful and Emigrants, barring any favour you may do your comrades. This has been written in the Book. (6)

Key Points

In Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, we read: The Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله established a bond of brotherhood (Aqd Ukhuwwah) among the Muslims (Ansar and Muhajirin), such that they inherited from each other like real brothers, because the immigrants (Muhajirin) had initially been separated from their homeland, wealth, and relatives. The bond of brotherhood compensated for these losses until the Ayah was revealed, which annulled such inheritance and stated: The criterion for inheritance is kinship. When the Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله command regarding movement for the Battle of Tabuk was issued, some said: We must seek permission from our parents. This sentence was revealed: «النّبى اولى بالمؤمنین».(۳۶۷) The Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, on the Day of Ghadir Khumm, when He appointed Hazrat Ali عليه السلام as His successor by the command of Allah, before introducing and appointing that Hazrat, stated the phrase: «ألستُ اولى بكم من انفسكم» and sought their affirmation: Do I not have priority over you, even concerning yourselves, according to the Quranic Ayah?! All said: Yes. Then the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «مَن كنتُ مولاه فهذا علىّ مولاه»(۳۶۸) The Prophet's priority over the believers is in governmental, individual, and all affairs. «النّبى اولى» Just as we read in Ayah 36 of this same Surah: For no believing man or woman is there any right of choice or option in the face of the Prophet's judgment. «ما كان لمؤمن ولا مؤمنة اذا قضى اللّه و رسوله امرا ان یكون لهم الخیرة» In many narrations, it is stated: The Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله also granted this priority to the infallible Imams علیهم السلام.(۳۶۹) In a Hadith, we read: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: «والذّى نفسى بیده لا یؤمن احدكم حتّى اكون اَحَبَّ الیه من نفسه و ماله و ولده والناس اجمعین» I swear by the God in Whose Hand is my soul, the faith of none of you is complete unless he loves me more than his own soul, his wealth, his children, and all people.(۳۷۰)

367) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 368) Bihar, Vol. 21, p. 387. 369) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 370) Tafsir Namuneh, quoting Fi Zilal.

Messages

1- The Prophet has complete guardianship (Wilayah) over all believers, and His guardianship over the people is superior to their own guardianship over their affairs. «النبىّ اولى بالمؤمنین» (In an Islamic society, religion must govern.)

2- The requirement of faith in Allah is the acceptance of the guardianship of the Messenger of Allah. «النّبى اولى»

3- Respect for the Prophet's wives is necessary. «ازواجه امّهاتهم» (Even after the Prophet's passing, no one has the right to marry the Prophet's wives.)

4- The status of the Prophet's wives as mothers does not cause them to inherit from the people. «و ازواجه امّهاتهم و اولوا الارحام بعضهم اولى ببعض»

5- Kinship and family ties bring priority. «اولوا الارحام بعضهم اولى ببعض»

6- Fixed laws of inheritance should not prevent attending to the situation of deprived friends. «الاّ ان تفعلوا الى اولیائكم معروفا»

7- The priority of relatives in inheritance comes after the execution of the will. «و اولوا الارحام بعضهم اولى ببعض... الاّ تفعلوا...» (Yes, if a person wills that a portion of his wealth be spent on his friends, the will must first be executed, and then the division should occur.)

8- We must take legal and judicial matters seriously in family affairs. «فى الكتاب مسطورا»

9- Recording and documenting affairs is a Divine act. «فى الكتاب مسطورا»

Ayah

وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِنَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ مِيثَـٰقَهُمْ وَمِنكَ وَمِن نُّوحٍ وَإِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَمُوسَىٰ وَعِيسَى ٱبْنِ مَرْيَمَ ۖ وَأَخَذْنَا مِنْهُم مِّيثَـٰقًا غَلِيظًا (۷)
[Recall] when We took a pledge from the prophets, and from you and from Noah and Abraham and Moses and Jesus son of Mary, and We took from them a solemn pledge, (7)

Key Points

Among the prophets, five individuals were Ulul-'Azm (possessors of resolve), meaning they possessed a Book and a Divine Law (Shari'ah), and enjoyed a special status near Allah. This Ayah specifically names them. However, the Prophet of Islam is mentioned before the rest, which indicates another level of greatness (371). Similarly, the mother of Prophet 'Isa (Jesus) is mentioned, which signifies the special position of Lady Maryam (Mary) and the miraculous birth of that Prophet.

371) Bihar, Vol. 5, p. 236.

Messages

1- For spiritual and educational preparation, familiarity with history and Divine traditions (Sunan) is necessary. (If We take a covenant from you, Our tradition and plan is that We have taken a covenant from all the prophets.) «أَخَذْنَا مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ»

2- If the Prophet has priority over the people, a heavy responsibility and covenant also rests upon Him. «أَخَذْنَا - مِيثَاقَهُمْ وَ مِنْكَ» Whoever holds a higher rank, his duty is harder. (Allah took a covenant from all the prophets, but He took a heavy covenant (Mithāqan Ghalīẓan) from the Ulul-'Azm prophets.) «مِيثَاقًا غَلِيظًا»

Ayah

لِّيَسْـَٔلَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ عَن صِدْقِهِمْ ۚ وَأَعَدَّ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (۸)
so that He may question the truthful concerning their truthfulness. And He has prepared for the faithless a painful punishment. (8)

Key Points

Perhaps the meaning of al-Sādiqīn (the truthful ones) is the Prophets, meaning Allah took a covenant from them so that He might question them on the Day of Judgment according to that same covenant and affirmation. And perhaps the meaning is that Allah took a strict covenant from the Prophets regarding the conveyance of the Divine revelation to the people, so that the people could be questioned about their truthfulness or disbelief. In the Quran, the truthful ones hold a special status, being mentioned with the expressions: «صدقوا ما عاهدوا اللّه علیه» (372), «اولئك هم الصّادقون» (373), and «كونوا مع الصّادقین» (374). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "If the truthful person is questioned about his truthfulness—what his motive was—and is rewarded accordingly, then what will be the state of the liar?!" (375) In this Ayah, instead of the believers being placed opposite the disbelievers, the truthful ones are mentioned. This means that truthfulness is an inseparable prerequisite of faith.

372) Al-Ahzab, 23. 373) Al-Hujurat, 15. 374) At-Tawbah, 119. 375) Tafasir Majma‘ al-Bayan and Burhan.

Messages

1- Divine covenant-taking is purposeful, and the goal is the separation of the truthful ones from the disbelievers. «لیسئل الصادقین - اعدّ للكافرین»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَةَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ جَآءَتْكُمْ جُنُودٌ فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحًا وَجُنُودًا لَّمْ تَرَوْهَا ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرًا (۹)
O you who have faith! Remember Allah’s blessing upon you when the hosts came at you, and We sent against them a gale and hosts whom you did not see, and Allah sees best what you do. (9)

Key Points

This Ayah and the sixteen Ayahs following it relate to the Battle of Al-Ahzab (the Confederates), which took place in the fifth year of the Hijra. In this war, where the Jews, polytheists, and hypocrites intended a coup and the occupation of Medina, the Muslims, based on the suggestion of Salman al-Farsi and the approval of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, dug a trench (khandaq) around Medina and stood firm against the enemy. In digging the trench, the Messenger of Allah Himself struck the first pickaxe into the ground, and every time a spark flew due to the pickaxe hitting a rock, the Prophet gave the glad tidings of Islam's victory over a region of the world. Allah aided the Muslims in this battle through the blowing of the wind and the descent of angels.

Messages

1- The condition for receiving Divine aid is faith and effort. «یا ایها الّذین آمنوا اذكروا نعمة اللّه» (The Muslims prepared for defense, dug the trench, and were then aided.)

2- Remembering blessings is commanded by Allah. «اذكروا نعمة اللّه» Remembering Divine aid raises the morale of the fighters.

3- Man is susceptible to forgetting blessings and needs reminder. «اذكروا نعمة اللّه»

4- Troops (جنود) must be answered with troops (جنود). «جاءتكم جنودٌ - فارسلنا ... جنوداً»

5- We must attribute victories to Allah.(376) «فارسلنا علیهم»

6- By the will of Allah, even the winds become soldiers. «فارسلنا علیهم ریحاً»

7- Angels both pray for the believers and give them glad tidings and aid. «فارسلنا - جنودا»

8- If Allah sees that we are fulfilling our duty, He sends down His aid upon us. «كان اللّه بما تعملون بصیراً» (376) Imam Khomeini قدس سره said regarding the liberation of Khorramshahr: "God liberated Khorramshahr."

Ayahs

إِذْ جَآءُوكُم مِّن فَوْقِكُمْ وَمِنْ أَسْفَلَ مِنكُمْ وَإِذْ زَاغَتِ ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرُ وَبَلَغَتِ ٱلْقُلُوبُ ٱلْحَنَاجِرَ وَتَظُنُّونَ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلظُّنُونَا۠ (۱٠)
When they came at you from above and below you, and when the eyes rolled [with fear] and the hearts leapt to the throats, and you entertained misgivings about Allah, (10)
هُنَالِكَ ٱبْتُلِىَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَزُلْزِلُوا۟ زِلْزَالًا شَدِيدًا (۱۱)
it was there that the faithful were tested and jolted with a severe agitation. (11)

Messages

1- You taste the flavor of Divine favors only when you visualize the bitter scenes for yourselves. «اذ جاؤكم - اذ زاغت»

2- Muslims must guard all the borders of their country. «فوقكم - اسفل منكم»

3- Psychological states affect the body. (Fear causes the eyes and heart to lose their normal function; the eyes staring blankly and the heart pounding rapidly are examples of this.) «زاغت الابصار و بلغت القلوب الحناجر»

4- Some believers harbor ill thoughts regarding the power of Allah when difficult events occur. «تظنّون باللّه»

5- The believer is subject to trial and test. «ابتُلى المؤمنون»

6- War, fear, terror, and difficult conditions are means of testing. «هنالك ابتلى»

7- During hardships, people are recognized and the ranks are separated. «هنالك ابتُلى» (In the battlefields, there was no sign of the pseudo-intellectuals and the hypocrites.)

8- The steadfastness of a person depends on the steadfastness of his spirit. (If the spirit is struck, the person becomes unstable.) «زلزلوا»

Ayah

وَإِذْ يَقُولُ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ وَٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ مَّا وَعَدَنَا ٱللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُۥٓ إِلَّا غُرُورًا (۱۲)
When the hypocrites, as well as those in whose hearts is a sickness, were saying, ‘Allah and His Apostle did not promise us [anything] except delusion.’ (12)

Messages

1- The propagandistic efforts of the hypocrites are continuous. «یقول المنافقون»

2- Discouraging the combatants and weakening their morale is a sign of deviation and hypocrisy. «ما وعدنا اللّه...»

3- Among the companions of the Prophet, some suffered from doubt and hesitation, and others suffered from hypocrisy and duplicity. «ما وعدنا اللّه و رسوله الاّ غرورا»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَت طَّآئِفَةٌ مِّنْهُمْ يَـٰٓأَهْلَ يَثْرِبَ لَا مُقَامَ لَكُمْ فَٱرْجِعُوا۟ ۚ وَيَسْتَـْٔذِنُ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُمُ ٱلنَّبِىَّ يَقُولُونَ إِنَّ بُيُوتَنَا عَوْرَةٌ وَمَا هِىَ بِعَوْرَةٍ ۖ إِن يُرِيدُونَ إِلَّا فِرَارًا (۱۳)
And when a group of them said, ‘O people of Yathrib! [This is] not a place for you, so go back!’ A group of them sought the Prophet’s permission [to leave the scene of battle], saying, ‘Our homes lie exposed [to the enemy],’ although they were not exposed. They only sought to flee. (13)

Messages

1- A group of hypocrites sought to weaken the morale of the believers: some through spreading rumors, «قالت طائفة... لا مقام لكم», and others by fleeing the front, «یستأذن فریق منهم».

2- We must not be heedless of the propaganda of the hypocrites used to discourage the fighters. «لا مقام لكم»

3- The commander of the war was the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله himself. «یستأذن فریق منهم النبىّ»

4- The armed forces must be so organized that no one has the right to leave the army without obtaining leave. «یستأذن فریق منهم النبىّ»

5- Mental and familial peace and security are so essential for a fighter that the hypocrites used concern over them as an excuse to abandon the front. «انّ بیوتنا عورة»

6- Whoever is not detached from his home and life cannot be successful. «انّ بیوتنا عورة»

7- The hypocrite is cowardly and prone to making excuses. (Due to lack of courage, he makes excuses instead of taking an explicit stance.) «بیوتنا عورة»

8- The enemy uses religion against religion. (The hypocrites' excuse was "protecting honor," which is a religious command.) «بیوتنا عورة»

9- Revelation exposes the wicked thoughts of the hypocrites and disgraces them. «و ما هى بعورة ان یریدون الاّ فرارا»

Ayahs

وَلَوْ دُخِلَتْ عَلَيْهِم مِّنْ أَقْطَارِهَا ثُمَّ سُئِلُوا۟ ٱلْفِتْنَةَ لَـَٔاتَوْهَا وَمَا تَلَبَّثُوا۟ بِهَآ إِلَّا يَسِيرًا (۱٤)
Had they been invaded from its flanks and had they been asked to apostatize, they would have done so with only a mild hesitation, (14)
وَلَقَدْ كَانُوا۟ عَـٰهَدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مِن قَبْلُ لَا يُوَلُّونَ ٱلْأَدْبَـٰرَ ۚ وَكَانَ عَهْدُ ٱللَّهِ مَسْـُٔولًا (۱٥)
though they had already pledged to Allah before that they would not turn their backs [to flee], and pledges given to Allah are accountable. (15)

Key Points

Not fleeing from the battlefield and war, «لا یولّون الادبار» (they will not turn their backs), is among the conditions of faith in Allah and the Prophet. Allah says: This covenant will certainly be questioned and inquired about. «كان عهد اللّه مسئولا»

Messages

1- The enemy seeks help from internal hypocrites to create discord (Fitnah). «سُئلوا الفتنة»

2- Hypocrites easily turn away from the truth, face the enemy, and instigate discord (Fitnah). «لاَتوها»

3- Accepting the requests of the disbelievers hastily and without thought is a sign of lack of identity (or instability). «سئلوا... ما تلبّثوا بها الاّ یسیرا»

4- Making a covenant is not what is important; loyalty is important. «عاهدوا اللّه... لا یولّون الادبار»

5- We should not take Allah's covenant lightly, for we will certainly be questioned and reproached. «كان عهد اللّه مسئولاً»

Ayah

قُل لَّن يَنفَعَكُمُ ٱلْفِرَارُ إِن فَرَرْتُم مِّنَ ٱلْمَوْتِ أَوِ ٱلْقَتْلِ وَإِذًا لَّا تُمَتَّعُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (۱٦)
Say, ‘Flight will not avail you, if you flee from death, or from being killed, and then you will be let to enjoy only for a little while.’ (16)

Messages

1- One cannot escape the inevitable decrees of Allah, such as death. «لن ینفعكم الفرار... من الموت او القتل»

2- Do not lose eternal happiness for the sake of fleeting gratification. «لا تمتّعون الاّ قلیلا»

Ayah

قُلْ مَن ذَا ٱلَّذِى يَعْصِمُكُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ إِنْ أَرَادَ بِكُمْ سُوٓءًا أَوْ أَرَادَ بِكُمْ رَحْمَةً ۚ وَلَا يَجِدُونَ لَهُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا (۱۷)
Say, ‘Who is it that can protect you from Allah if He desires to cause you harm or desires to grant you His mercy?’ Besides Allah they will not find for themselves any protector or helper. (17)

Key Points

The will of Allah concerning the occurrence of hardship and difficulty for man means that man, by his own choice and the performance of unworthy deeds (such as fleeing from battle), drags himself toward the fire of divine wrath. And since all affairs take place by the Divine Will, these hardships are attributed to Allah.

Messages

1- Awaken consciences by posing questions. «قل من ... یعصمكم من اللّه»

2- Mercy and punishment, bitterness and sweetness, reach man within the orbit of Allah's Will. «اراد بكم سوءاً - اراد بكم رحمة» (Of course, Allah's will for harm or mercy is based on wisdom.)

Ayah

۞ قَدْ يَعْلَمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُعَوِّقِينَ مِنكُمْ وَٱلْقَآئِلِينَ لِإِخْوَٰنِهِمْ هَلُمَّ إِلَيْنَا ۖ وَلَا يَأْتُونَ ٱلْبَأْسَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (۱۸)
Allah knows those of you who discourage others, and those who say to their brethren, ‘Come to us!’ and take little part in the battle, (18)

Key Points

Mu'awwiq (مُعَوِّق) means hinderer (or deterrent), and ba's (بأس) means hardship, the field of battle, and Jihad. The Quran refers to various types of brotherhood: A: Natural brotherhood, which is through parents. B: Religious brotherhood, which is achieved through shared faith: «انّما المؤمنون اخوة» (377) C: Political and partisan brotherhood, which is referred to in this Ayah. D: Behavioral brotherhood, which is achieved through following (a path). For instance, the extravagant (spendthrifts) are introduced as brothers of Satan. «انّ المبذّرین كانوا اخوان الشیاطین» (378) The meaning of the phrase «القائلین لاخوانهم» (those who say to their brothers) is the invitation by the hypocrites to the weak-faithed Muslims to abandon the front (battlefield).

377) Hujurat, 10. 378) Isra, 27.

Messages

1- Allah is aware of all the movements and malicious propaganda of the enemies. «یعلم اللّه»

2- Cowardly hypocrites who themselves flee the front prevent others from attending the front. «المعوّقین... القائلین»

3- Any form of discouraging the fighters is a sin in the sight of Allah. «یعلم اللّه... القائلین... هلمّ الینا»

4- Going to the front is not important; remaining at the front is important. «لا یأتون البأس الاّ قلیلا»

Ayah

أَشِحَّةً عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ فَإِذَا جَآءَ ٱلْخَوْفُ رَأَيْتَهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ تَدُورُ أَعْيُنُهُمْ كَٱلَّذِى يُغْشَىٰ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ ٱلْمَوْتِ ۖ فَإِذَا ذَهَبَ ٱلْخَوْفُ سَلَقُوكُم بِأَلْسِنَةٍ حِدَادٍ أَشِحَّةً عَلَى ٱلْخَيْرِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا۟ فَأَحْبَطَ ٱللَّهُ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرًا (۱۹)
grudging you [their help]. So when there is panic, you see them looking at you, with their eyes rolling like someone fainting at death. Then, when the panic is over, they scald you with [their] sharp tongues in their greed for the spoils. They never have had faith. So Allah has made their works fail, and that is easy for Allah. (19)

Key Points

The word 'ashihhah' (أشحّة) is the plural of 'shahiih' (شحیح), derived from 'shuhh' (شُحّ), meaning stinginess (or avarice) accompanied by greed. 'Salaqūkum' (سلقوكم) is derived from 'salq' (سلق), meaning to overcome or defeat. That is, they seek to overcome and defeat the Muslims through their propaganda and sharp tongues (or verbal abuse). 'Ḥidād' (حِداد) is the plural of 'ḥadīd' (حدید), meaning sharp and harsh (or severe).

Messages

1- The hypocrites are never your well-wishers; during times of danger, they are stingy regarding aid and presence on the battlefield, «اشحّة علیكم» and after the danger is removed, they are greedy for collecting spoils. «اشحّة على الخیر»

2- The hypocrites perceive hardships as coming from the Prophet and the Muslims. «فاذا جاء الخوف... ینظرون الیك»

3- Hypocrites lose their composure during critical situations. «فاذا جاء الخوف... تدور اعینهم»

4- Psychological states affect a person's appearance. «تدور اعینهم»

5- Hypocrites are low on action and high on talk. During war and hardship, they are the most cowardly people, and during peace, they are the most demanding people. «اشحّة علیكم - اشحّة على الخیر»

6- Fear, stinginess, sharp tongues (verbal abuse), and undue expectation are prominent signs of the hypocrites. «اشحّة - تدور اعینهم - السنة حداد»

7- The nullification (ḥabṭ) of the deeds of sinners is based on wisdom and the performance of the individual himself. «لم یؤمنوا فاحبط اللّه اعمالهم»

8- Hypocrisy is the cause of the nullification and ruin of deeds. «احبط اللّه اعمالهم»

9- The behavior of the hypocrites (stinginess, sharp tongues, discouraging people from joining the front, etc.) cannot create a problem for the Will of Allah. «كان ذلك على اللّه یسیرا»

Ayah

يَحْسَبُونَ ٱلْأَحْزَابَ لَمْ يَذْهَبُوا۟ ۖ وَإِن يَأْتِ ٱلْأَحْزَابُ يَوَدُّوا۟ لَوْ أَنَّهُم بَادُونَ فِى ٱلْأَعْرَابِ يَسْـَٔلُونَ عَنْ أَنۢبَآئِكُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ كَانُوا۟ فِيكُم مَّا قَـٰتَلُوٓا۟ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (۲٠)
They suppose the confederates have not left yet, and were the confederates to come [again], they would wish they were in the desert with the Bedouins asking about your news, and if they were with you they would fight but a little. (20)

Key Points

The city-dwelling Arab is called "Arabi," and the desert-dwelling Arab is called "A'rabi." The word "Bādūn" is the plural of "Bādī" and refers to someone who resides in the desert or wilderness.

Messages

1- Cowardly and weak-faithed individuals always imagine the enemy to be stronger than the Muslims. (The hypocrites thought that the confederates [Ahzab] of the Jews and polytheists would not disperse until they had conquered Medina.) «یحسبون الاحزاب لم یذهبوا»

2- Loss of morale deprives a person of the ability to understand realities. «یحسبون... لم یذهبوا»

3- Weak-faithed individuals prefer living in uncivilized societies over living in a religious community. «یودّوا لو انّهم بادون»

4- Instead of being present on the social scene, weak-faithed individuals are preoccupied with reading newspapers and listening to radio news. «یسئلون عن انبائكم»

5- Weak-faithed individuals are not people of battle and steadfastness. «ما قاتلوا الاّ قلیلا»

Ayah

لَّقَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ فِى رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ لِّمَن كَانَ يَرْجُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱلْيَوْمَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَ وَذَكَرَ ٱللَّهَ كَثِيرًا (۲۱)
There is certainly a good exemplar for you in the Apostle of Allah—for those who look forward to Allah and the Last Day, and remember Allah much. (21)

Key Points

The term Uswah (اُسوة) is used regarding emulation (ta'assī) and following others in good deeds. This word appears twice in the Quran concerning two great Prophets: once regarding Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) عليه السلام and the other regarding the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله. Interestingly, the Uswah (exemplary status) of Prophet Ibrahim is related to dissociation from polytheism (shirk) and polytheists, while the Uswah of the Prophet of Islam is mentioned in verses concerning steadfastness against the enemy. The role of the Prophet in the Battle of the Confederates (Ahzab) is a model for commanders: leading the army, giving hope, digging the trench, raising inspiring slogans, being close to the enemy, and showing resilience. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: During the heat of battle, we would seek refuge behind the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and He was closer to the enemy than any of us. «اتّقینا برسول اللّه صلى الله عليه وآله فلم یكن منّا اقرب الى العدوّ منه»(379) This verse is among the verses concerning the Battle of the Confederates; however, the Prophet's exemplary status is not limited to warfare, and the Prophet is the best model for believers in all fields.

379) Nahj al-Balaghah, Saying 260.

Messages

1- Do not doubt the Prophet's exemplary status. «لقد»

2- The Prophet's exemplary status is permanent. «كان»

3- The Prophet's speech, conduct, and meaningful silence are authoritative (hujjah). «لكم فى رسول اللّه اسوة حسنة»

4- Introducing a model is one of the methods of education/training. «لكم - اسوة»

5- We must introduce good models so that people do not turn to false/substitute models. «رسول اللّه اسوة»

6- The best method of propagation is practical invitation. «اُسوة»

7- Only those whose hearts are filled with faith and the remembrance of Allah can take the Noble Prophet as a model. «لمن كان یرجوا اللّه ...»

8- No matter how dear the models are, a person must not forget Allah. «رسول اللّه اُسوة - ذكر اللّه كثیرا»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا رَءَا ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ٱلْأَحْزَابَ قَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَا مَا وَعَدَنَا ٱللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُۥ وَصَدَقَ ٱللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُۥ ۚ وَمَا زَادَهُمْ إِلَّآ إِيمَـٰنًا وَتَسْلِيمًا (۲۲)
But when the faithful saw the confederates, they said, ‘This is what Allah and His Apostle had promised us, and Allah and His Apostle were true.’ And it only increased them in faith and submission. (22)

Key Points

We read in Ayah 12 that the hypocrites and the faint-hearted called the promises of the Prophet a delusion, but in this Ayah, the believers consider these promises real and truthful. It was stated two Ayahs prior that a group of weak-faithed individuals wished that they were in areas far from the front line during the enemy attack, but in this Ayah, He says: when the believers see the enemy, their faith and submission to Allah increase.

Messages

1- Faith is the source of strength. «رءا المؤمنون الاحزاب قالوا...»

2- Hypocrites imagine an enemy that is not present to be present; «یحسبون الاحزاب لم یذهبوا» but believers consider the existing and attacking enemies insignificant. «ولمّا رءا المؤمنون... - هذا ما وعدنا اللّه»

3- The attack by united enemies is not unexpected for the believer. «هذا ما وعدنا اللّه»

4- The word of the Messenger of Allah is the same as the word of Allah. «وعدنا اللّه و رسوله»

5- The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله had informed the Muslims about the occurrence of the Battle of the Confederates (Ahzab). «هذا ما وعدنا اللّه و رسوله»

6- Believers consider the fulfillment of the promises of Allah and His Messenger certain. «وعدنا اللّه و رسوله - صدق اللّه و رسوله»

7- The front line and war are a platform for growth and progress for the believer. «ما زادهم الاّ ایماناً و تسلیماً»

8- Believers are not afraid of the number or organization of the enemies. «ما زادهم الاّ ایمانا»

9- Faith (Iman) and submission (Taslim) have degrees and levels. «ما زادهم الاّ ایماناً و تسلیماً»

10- The fulfillment of the promises of Allah and the Messenger increases the faith of the believers. «و لمّا رءا المؤمنون... ما زادهم الاّ ایماناً و تسلیماً»

Ayah

مِّنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ رِجَالٌ صَدَقُوا۟ مَا عَـٰهَدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ عَلَيْهِ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مَّن قَضَىٰ نَحْبَهُۥ وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَنتَظِرُ ۖ وَمَا بَدَّلُوا۟ تَبْدِيلًا (۲۳)
Among the faithful are men who fulfill what they have pledged to Allah: there are some among them who have fulfilled their pledge, and some of them who still wait, and they have not changed in the least, (23)

Key Points

The word "نحب" (naḥb) has many meanings, but here it signifies a vow, covenant, or pledge, the fulfillment of which might lead to death or a great danger. In contrast to the group that desired a life of comfort, "یودّوا لو انّهم بادون فى الاعراب" (They wish that they were in the desert among the Bedouins) (380), another group awaits martyrdom. "و منهم من ینتظر" (And among them is he who awaits). Imam Husayn عليه السلام recited this Ayah in Karbala when bidding farewell to his companions and sometimes over the heads of the martyrs. (381)

(380) Al-Ahzab, 20 (381) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq.

Messages

1- Not all companions of the Prophet and believers are of the same rank. "من المؤمنین" (Among the believers).

2- Narrating the virtues of others is one method of education (or moral training). "من المؤمنین رجال ..." (Among the believers are men...).

3- Defending the truth up to the point of martyrdom is a sign of sincerity in faith. "صدقوا ما عاهدوا اللّه" (They have been true to what they covenanted with Allah).

4- Commitment is mandatory, and acting upon it is a sign of sincerity. "صدقوا ما عاهدوا" (They have been true to what they covenanted).

5- Honoring the martyrs is a Quranic message and lesson. "فمنهم من قضى نحبه" (So among them is he who has fulfilled his pledge).

6- Awaiting martyrdom is also a virtue. "و منهم من ینتظر" (And among them is he who awaits). Readiness to meet Allah is among the characteristics of the believers.

7- The door to martyrdom is open. "و منهم من ینتظر" (And among them is he who awaits).

8- The martyrdom of some believers does not cause other believers to retreat or become disheartened. (Even though the believer witnesses the martyrdom of his friends and loved ones, he himself still awaits martyrdom.) "و منهم من ینتظر" (And among them is he who awaits).

9- True believers adhere firmly to their covenant with Allah, and nothing, not even the martyrdom of friends and loved ones, changes their spirit. "و ما بدّلوا تبدیلا" (And they have not altered [their commitment] in the least alteration).

Ayah

لِّيَجْزِىَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ بِصِدْقِهِمْ وَيُعَذِّبَ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ إِن شَآءَ أَوْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (۲٤)
that Allah may reward the true for their truthfulness, and punish the hypocrites, if He wishes, or accept their repentance. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (24)

Key Points

The word Tawbah (repentance), if used with the preposition ila (إلى), refers to the repentance of the servants towards Allah. If it is used with ala (على), it refers to the attention and grace of Allah upon the servants. «یتوب علیهم»

Messages

1- Bitter and difficult events are purposeful, so that talents may blossom and man may attain reward through effort and striving. «لیجزى»

2- By rewarding the truthful, we should encourage others towards sincerity. «لیجزى اللّه الصادقین بصدقهم»

3- Salvation lies in truthfulness. «لیجزى ... بصدقهم»

4- Receiving reward is based on sincerity in action. «بصدقهم»

5- Glad tidings (reward) precede warning (punishment). «لیجزى - یعذّب»

6- No deed is without reward. (Neither the truthfulness of the truthful nor the hypocrisy of the hypocrites.) «لیجزى - یعذّب»

7- The grace of Allah is not far even from the hypocrites, provided that they themselves desire it. «او یتوب علیهم»

8- Allah's acceptance of repentance is accompanied by His grace. «غفوراً رحیما»

Ayah

وَرَدَّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِغَيْظِهِمْ لَمْ يَنَالُوا۟ خَيْرًا ۚ وَكَفَى ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ٱلْقِتَالَ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ قَوِيًّا عَزِيزًا (۲٥)
Allah sent back the faithless in their rage, without their attaining any advantage, and Allah spared the faithful of fighting, and Allah is all-strong, all-mighty. (25)

Key Points

In the Battle of al-Ahzab (the Confederates), as soon as Hadrat Ali عليه السلام killed 'Amr ibn 'Abd Wudd, the famous Arab champion who had crossed the trench, they calculated their situation and retreated.

Messages

1- We should not fear the unity of the enemies. «ردّ اللّه الّذین كفروا» Although three groups—the polytheists, the Jews, and the hypocrites—united against the Muslims in the Battle of al-Ahzab, they returned bewildered without achieving victory or spoils.

2- The ups and downs of wars are not outside the divine Will. «ردّ اللّه - كفى اللّه»

3- In the Battle of al-Ahzab (the Trench), unseen divine assistance was at work. «ردّ اللّه - كفى اللّه»

4- Sometimes one must speak from the opponent's perspective. «لم ینالوا خیراً» Victory over Islam and the acquisition of spoils were considered 'good' (khayr) from the viewpoint of the disbelievers.

5- The goal of the enemies' wars is to achieve victory, acquire spoils, expand territory, or protect their own interests. «لم ینالوا خیراً» But the goal of Islamic wars is «احدى الحسنیین» (one of the two good things) (382): that is, either fulfilling a religious duty, inviting and guiding the unaware, and removing obstacles to the spread of truth, or attaining martyrdom and meeting the Truth (Allah).

6- The source of the Muslims' honor and power is Allah. «كفى اللّه - كان اللّه قویّا عزیزا» (382) At-Tawbah, 52.

Ayahs

وَأَنزَلَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَـٰهَرُوهُم مِّنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مِن صَيَاصِيهِمْ وَقَذَفَ فِى قُلُوبِهِمُ ٱلرُّعْبَ فَرِيقًا تَقْتُلُونَ وَتَأْسِرُونَ فَرِيقًا (۲٦)
And He dragged down from their strongholds those who had backed them from among the People of the Book, and He cast terror into their hearts, [so that] you killed a part of them, and took captive [another] part. (26)
وَأَوْرَثَكُمْ أَرْضَهُمْ وَدِيَـٰرَهُمْ وَأَمْوَٰلَهُمْ وَأَرْضًا لَّمْ تَطَـُٔوهَا ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرًا (۲۷)
He bequeathed you their land, their houses and their possessions, and a territory you had not trodden, and Allah has power over all things. (27)

Key Points

Ṣayāṣī (صیاصى) is the plural of ṣīṣah (صیصة), meaning strong fortresses. It is also used for any defensive means, such as the horn of a cow or the spurs on a rooster's leg. This Ayah refers to the Battle of Banu Qurayzah. The Jews of Banu Qurayzah, imagining victory in the Battle of al-Ahzab (the Confederates), broke their covenant with the Muslims and aided the polytheists of Mecca during the battle as the enemy's fifth column. However, they were disgraced, defeated, and surrendered. There were three Jewish tribes in Medina named: Banu Qaynuqa‘, Banu Naḍīr, and Banu Qurayzah, all of whom had covenants with the Muslims. In the second year (of the Hijra), Banu Qaynuqa‘ broke the covenant, fought the Muslims, were defeated, and left Medina, heading towards Syria (Sham). In the fourth year, Banu Naḍīr fought and were defeated, heading towards Syria, thus vacating Khaybar and Medina. In the fifth year, Banu Qurayzah broke their covenant and cooperated with the polytheists. With the defeat of the Confederates, anxiety overcame them. After the victory over the Confederates, the Muslims besieged their fortresses and, after 25 days, drove them out of Medina. Thus, Medina was forever cleansed of the impurity of the covenant-breaking Jewish tribes. (383) The war against Banu Qurayzah brought blessings, including: A: The last group of Jews permanently left Medina. B: Abundant wealth from the enemy fell to the Muslims. C: The position of the Muslims was solidified, and it became clear that anyone who breaks a covenant with them will face misfortune.

383) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

  1. One must not be heedless of the enemy's infiltrating forces and fifth column. «و انزل الّذین ظاهروهم»

  2. Peaceful coexistence with the People of the Book is conditional upon them not aiding the opponents (of Islam). «و انزل الّذین ظاهروهم من اهل الكتاب»

  3. Victory comes from Allah. «و انزل»

  4. The enemy's strong and high fortresses are conquerable. «و انزل ... من صیاصیهم»

  5. Although the Jewish People of the Book shared common ground with the Muslims in monotheism and belief in the Resurrection, they rose up in support of the polytheists. «ظاهروهم من اهل الكتاب»

  6. Do not only focus on the enemy facing you directly, but also pay attention to their supporters. «ظاهروهم»

  7. One of the unseen aids of Allah in wars is casting terror into the hearts of the enemies. «قذف فى قلوبهم الرّعب»

  8. The morale of the combatant plays a primary role in the defeat or victory of the front. «فى قلوبهم الرّعب... تقتلون و تأسرون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ قُل لِّأَزْوَٰجِكَ إِن كُنتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتَهَا فَتَعَالَيْنَ أُمَتِّعْكُنَّ وَأُسَرِّحْكُنَّ سَرَاحًا جَمِيلًا (۲۸)
O Prophet! Say to your wives, ‘If you desire the life of the world and its glitter, come, I will provide for you and release you in a graceful manner. (28)

Key Points

Following the acquisition of war spoils by the Muslims, the wives of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله sought to expand their lifestyle and requested means and facilities of comfort from him. The Messenger of Allah opposed their request and distanced Himself from them for a period of one month until the verses under discussion were revealed.

Messages

  1. The life of the leader of the Muslims must be simple. (Religious and social status creates a specific duty for a person.) «ان كنتن تردن الحیاة الدنیا... اُسرحكنّ»
  2. The leader of the Muslims should not be influenced by the inappropriate demands of his wife. «قل لازواجك»
  3. The increase in the income of the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal) should not raise the expectations of the leader's relatives. «قل لازواجك»
  4. Not only the leader of the Islamic society, but also his dependents are under the scrutiny and attention of the people. «قل لازواجك»
  5. Divine responsibilities supersede familial responsibilities. (Sometimes, due to the sanctity of leadership, one must overlook emotional and family matters.) «قل لازواجك»
  6. A woman is free; she can remain with a simple life accompanied by happiness, or she can choose another path. «ان كنتنّ تردن... اسرحكنّ»
  7. In the face of an inappropriate demand from a wife, declare your definitive opinion clearly and explicitly. «ان كنتنّ تردن» (Eliminate undue expectations with firmness, yet accompanied by kindness and justice.)
  8. When accessing the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal) and spoils, we must prevent the undue expectations of others by dealing decisively with family and close relatives. «ان كنتنّ... فتعالین امتّعكنّ»
  9. If a woman cannot tolerate a simple and healthy life, divorce her with justice and fairness, and do not force her to continue the marriage. «ان كنتنّ... امتعكنّ و اسرّحكنّ»
  10. The danger of worldliness threatens even the household of prophethood. «ان كنتنّ تردن...»
  11. Enjoying the gold and adornments of the world is not forbidden (haram), but it is not befitting the status of the household of prophethood. «فتعالین امتّعكنّ»
  12. If you are compelled to divorce, carry out the separation with goodness and justice. «اسرّحكنّ سراحاً جمیلا»

Ayah

وَإِن كُنتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَٱلدَّارَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْمُحْسِنَـٰتِ مِنكُنَّ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (۲۹)
But if you desire Allah and His Apostle and the abode of the Hereafter, then Allah has indeed prepared a great reward for the virtuous among you.’ (29)

Messages

1- Seeking the world and seeking the Hereafter cannot be combined. «تردن الحیاة الدنیا... تردن اللّه...» Of course, enjoying and making lawful use of blessings does not contradict seeking the Hereafter.

2- Man must choose one between attachment to the fleeting world and the eternal Hereafter. «و ان كنتنّ ...»

3- Contentment, asceticism (zuhd), and simplicity alone are not factors for salvation; good deeds are also necessary. «تردن اللّه و رسوله والدّار الآخرة ... للمحسنات منكنّ»

4- We must consider the divine promises certain. «اعدّ»

5- Allah considers simple living for the families of religious leaders to be a good and virtuous matter. «اعدّ للمحسنات منكنّ»

6- Being the wife of the Prophet is not perfection; being virtuous (a doer of good) is necessary. «للمحسنات منكنّ»

7- Not all the wives of the Prophet were equal. «للمحسنات منكنّ»

8- Those who forgo the meager world will attain a great reward. «اجراً عظیما»

Ayah

يَـٰنِسَآءَ ٱلنَّبِىِّ مَن يَأْتِ مِنكُنَّ بِفَـٰحِشَةٍ مُّبَيِّنَةٍ يُضَـٰعَفْ لَهَا ٱلْعَذَابُ ضِعْفَيْنِ ۚ وَكَانَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرًا (۳٠)
O wives of the Prophet! Whoever of you commits a gross indecency, her punishment shall be doubled, and that is easy for Allah. (30)

Key Points

The verses that address the wives of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله are conveyed through the station of Prophethood, and not as a direct address or revelation to them personally, unlike Lady Maryam (Mary), who was addressed directly: «یا مریم» (384) Sins are of two types: major (Kabirah) and minor (Saghirah). The source of this classification is the blessed verse: «اِن تجتنبوا كبائر ما تنهون عنه نكفّر عنكم سیّئاتكم» (385), meaning: if you avoid the major sins which you are forbidden from, We will remit your minor sins (Sayyi’at). However, in certain instances, a minor sin takes the ruling of a major sin, such as sinning during a specific time, like the month of Ramadan, where the crime and its punishment are greater; or sinning in a specific place; repeating the sin and insisting upon it; sinning accompanied by joy; sinning openly; and the sin committed by prominent and exemplary individuals, such as the wives of the Prophet. In any case, secondary factors can influence the sin and its punishment. It is mentioned in narrations that the sins of great figures are judged differently; even if a Prophet commits a Tark al-Awla (leaving the preferable), they are disciplined. A famous Hadith states: «یُغفَر للجاهل سبعون ذنباً قبل أن یُغفَر للعالم ذنبٌ واحد» (386) Seventy sins of the ignorant person are forgiven before a single sin of the scholar is forgiven.

384) Aal-i-Imran, 42 - 43. 385) An-Nisa, 31. 386) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 47.

Messages

1- Lineage and status have no effect on punishment and reward, and every transgressor must be penalized. «یا نساء النبى ...»

2- Being the wife of the Prophet does not necessitate infallibility (‘Ismah). «من یأت منكنّ بفاحشة» (Indeed, being in the Prophet’s house does not insure a person; it is the school of thought [the Maktab] that insures a person.)

3- The reckoning for public transgressions is separate from that of hidden sins. «مبیّنه»

4- An evil deed inherently brings punishment. «یُضاعف» (It is doubled passively), and He did not say: «نضاعف» (We double actively).

5- The severity and leniency of punishment and reward are based on the individual and social effects of the action. «نساء النبى... ضعفین»

6- Those who hold religious and social prominence and whose actions serve as an example for others, if they sin, their punishment is greater than that of others. «نساء النبى... ضعفین»

7- The family and relatives of officials in the Islamic system must know that if they commit a transgression, they will not only fail to be acquitted, but they will receive several times the punishment of others. «یا نساء النبى... العذاب ضعفین»

8- If the punishment for the transgressing relatives of an official is several times greater, how severe is the punishment for the transgressing official himself? «نساء النبى... العذاب ضعفین» (Regarding the punishment for the Prophet’s own transgression, the Quran states: «لو تقوّل بعض الاقاویل... لقطعنا منه الوتین» (387) If He were to attribute some false sayings to Us, We would certainly cut off his life-vein.)

9- Punishing transgressors is easy for Allah. «على اللّه یسیر» 387) Haaqqah, 44 - 46.

Ayah

۞ وَمَن يَقْنُتْ مِنكُنَّ لِلَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَتَعْمَلْ صَـٰلِحًا نُّؤْتِهَآ أَجْرَهَا مَرَّتَيْنِ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لَهَا رِزْقًا كَرِيمًا (۳۱)
But whoever of you is obedient to Allah and His Apostle and acts righteously, We shall give her a twofold reward, and We will have in store for her a noble provision. (31)

Key Points

Qunūt (قنوت) means obedience accompanied by humility, respect, and submission. A person said to Imam Sajjad عليه السلام: "انّكم اهل بیت مغفورٌ لكم" (You, the Household of the Prophet, are forgiven). The Imam عليه السلام became angry and said: "The same verses apply to us that apply to the wives of the Prophet; if we commit a sin, our punishment is doubled, and if we are righteous, our reward is doubled." Then His Holiness recited this Ayah and the preceding Ayah. (388) The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: The strike of Ali عليه السلام (and the defeat of Amr ibn Abd Wudd) on the Day of the Battle of the Trench (Khandaq) is superior to the worship of jinn and mankind. "ضربة علىّ یوم الخندق افضل من عبادة الثّقلین" (389)

388) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 389) Al-Ghadir, Vol. 7, p. 206.

Messages

1- Encouragement and threat are effective when presented together. «من یأت منكنّ بفاحشة - و من یقنت»

2- Obedience is valuable only when it is based on knowledge and love, and accompanied by humility. «یقنت... للّه و رسوله»

3- We must obey both the commands of Allah and the commands of His Messenger. «یقنت... للّه و رسوله»

4- Performing a single righteous deed is not enough; its continuity is necessary. «تعمل صالحاً»

5- Being the wife of the Prophet is not sufficient; action is required. «تعمل صالحا»

6- The size or magnitude of the deed is not important; its righteousness is the condition. «صالحاً» (righteous deed) is indefinite (nakerah).

7- To encourage His servants, Allah attributes the reward to Himself. «نُؤتها - اعتدنا»

8- The reward and recompense vary based on the status and the individual and social effects of the deed. «نُؤتها اجرها مرّتین»

Ayah

يَـٰنِسَآءَ ٱلنَّبِىِّ لَسْتُنَّ كَأَحَدٍ مِّنَ ٱلنِّسَآءِ ۚ إِنِ ٱتَّقَيْتُنَّ فَلَا تَخْضَعْنَ بِٱلْقَوْلِ فَيَطْمَعَ ٱلَّذِى فِى قَلْبِهِۦ مَرَضٌ وَقُلْنَ قَوْلًا مَّعْرُوفًا (۳۲)
O wives of the Prophet! You are not like other women: if you are wary [of Allah], do not be complaisant in your speech, lest he in whose heart is a sickness should aspire; speak honourable words. (32)

Messages

1- The status of the families of religious leaders is separate from others, and they must be more careful of their behavior than anyone else. «یا نساء النبى لستن كاحد من النساء» (Social position brings limitations.)

2- When the wives of the Prophet, most of whom were old and elderly and lived simple lives, are warned not to speak flirtatiously or coquettishly, young and beautiful women must take stock of their own conduct. «یا نساء النبّى ... فلا تخضعن ...»

3- A woman speaking flirtatiously is a lack of piety. «اِن اتّقیتنّ فلا تخضعن بالقول»

4- The Quran also outlines etiquette for speaking. «فلا تخضعن بالقول»

5- The mere covetousness of the sick-hearted is enough reason for a woman to control herself, even if they do not have serious intentions (of committing sin). «لاتخضعن... فیطمع»

6- Corrupt individuals do not refrain from coveting even sacred figures/places. «یا نساء النبّى ... فیطمع الّذى فى قلبه مرض»

7- A woman's speech and behavior must not be provocative. «فیطمع الّذى فى قلبه مرض»

8- Even in the Prophetic society, not all individuals are sound (healthy). «فیطمع الّذى فى قلبه مرض»

9- We must not do anything that causes even a single person to fall into sin and corruption. «الّذى فى قلبه مرض» and He did not say «الّذین فى قلوبهم»

10- Leering and lustfulness are spiritual diseases. «فى قلبه مرض»

11- Although the person who is sick-hearted has a problem, «فى قلبه مرض» I must not do anything that provokes him. «فلا تخضعن... فیطمع»

12- Speak in such a way that both the content is good «قولاً معروفا» and the manner is sound. «فلا تخضعن بالقول»

Several important principles in forbidding evil (based on the Ayah)

  1. We should utilize the family status of individuals. «یا نساء النّبىّ»

  2. We should utilize the social status of individuals. «لَستُنّ كاَحدٍ من النساء»

  3. We should use titles that are beloved by all. «اِن اتّقیتُنّ»

  4. We should start with the simplest action. «فلا تخضعن بالقول»

  5. We should start with the elders and those close (to the leadership). «یا نساء النّبىّ»

  6. We should forbid the sources of sins. «فلا تَخضعنَ بالقول فیطمع»

  7. We should not overlook small behaviors and deviations. «فلا تَخضعنَ بالقول»

  8. We should explain the consequences and outcomes of corruption and deviation. «فیطمع الّذى فى قلبه مرض»

  9. Enjoining good and forbidding evil should be side by side. «فلا تخضعن - قلن»

  10. We should act even to prevent an evil from affecting a single individual. «فیطمع الّذى» He did not say: «فیطمع الّذین»

  11. We must fight against the desire and longing for sin, let alone the sin itself. «فیطمع»

Ayah

وَقَرْنَ فِى بُيُوتِكُنَّ وَلَا تَبَرَّجْنَ تَبَرُّجَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِيَّةِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ ۖ وَأَقِمْنَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتِينَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَأَطِعْنَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنكُمُ ٱلرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ ٱلْبَيْتِ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيرًا (۳۳)
Stay in your houses and do not flaunt your finery like the former [days of pagan] ignorance. Maintain the prayer and pay the zakat, and obey Allah and His Apostle. Indeed Allah desires to repel all impurity from you, O People of the Household, and purify you with a thorough purification. (33)

Key Points

«قَرْنَ» (Qarn) is derived from «وقار» (Waqar - dignity/serenity) or «قرار» (Qarar - settlement/staying put), and the result of both meanings is the same. That is, do not leave your homes for the purpose of showing off. «تبّرج» (Tabarruj) is derived from «بُرج» (Burj - tower) meaning showing off, just as a tower has a special prominence among other buildings.

The First Ignorance (Jahiliyyah al-Ula) indicates the emergence of the Other Ignorance (Jahiliyyah al-Ukhra), which we witness today. It is also mentioned in a Hadith: «ستكون الجاهلیّة الاخرى» (The Other Ignorance will soon come). (390)

If in the Ignorance of that day, girls were buried alive, today, through abortion, both girls and boys are destroyed. If in the Ignorance of that day there was murder and killing, today, in major and global wars, millions are slaughtered. The Ignorance of that day lacked schools and crimes were committed; today, despite thousands of universities, the most important human rights are trampled upon. In the Ignorance of that day, corruption and fornication were crimes; but today, in the most advanced countries of the world, homosexuality is legal. (391)

Points regarding the Ayah of Purification «اِنَّما یُریدُ اللّهُ لیُذهِبَ عنكْم الرِّجْسَ اَهلَ البَیتِ وَ یُطهِّرَكُمْ تَطهیراً»

  • The word «اِنّما» indicates that the noble Ayah speaks about an exceptional divine gift concerning the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام.
  • The intent of «یُرید» (He intends) is the creative Will (Irada Takwini), because the legislative Will (Irada Tashri'i) of Allah is the purity of all people, not just the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله.
  • The intent of «رجس» (Rijs) is every kind of impurity, both apparent and hidden. (392)
  • The intent of «اهل البیت» (Ahl al-Bayt) is not the entire family of the Prophet, but specific individuals whose names are mentioned in Shi'a and Sunni narrations. This group consists of: Ali, Fatimah, Hasan, and Husayn علیهم السلام.

Question

What is the proof for this exclusivity? And why is this part of the Ayah not about the wives of the Prophet or His entire family?

Answer

  1. Narrations have been transmitted from the Prophet's own wives, even from Aisha and Umm Salamah, that they asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: Are we also part of the Ahl al-Bayt? The Prophet replied: No.
  2. In numerous narrations, we read: The Prophet drew a cloak (Kisa) over these five individuals (one of whom was the Prophet Himself) and said: These are my Ahl al-Bayt, and He did not permit others to enter.
  3. To establish the exclusivity of the Ahl al-Bayt to these five individuals, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, for a period of six months (and according to some narrations, eight or nine months), would pass by the house of Fatimah علیها السلام at the time of the Fajr prayer and say: «الصّلاة یا اهل البیت انّما یرید اللّه لیذهب عنكم الرّجس اهل البیت و یطهركم تطهیرا»

In the book Ihqaq al-Haqq, more than seventy Hadiths from well-known Sunni sources are mentioned regarding the exclusivity of the noble Ayah to these five individuals, and in the book Shawahid al-Tanzil, which is a famous Sunni work, more than one hundred and thirty narrations are cited on the same topic. (393)

In any case, this Ayah does not include the Prophet's wives because: They sometimes committed sins. In Surah At-Tahrim, we read: The Prophet confided a secret to one of His wives, and she did not keep the trust and told it to another. The Quran called this act a sin, saying: «ان تتوبا اِلى اللّه فقد صغت قلوبكما» (394) Aisha, who was the Prophet's wife, initiated the Battle of the Camel against Imam Ali علیه السلام and later regretted it, despite the fact that a great number of Muslims were killed in that war.

A few questions

  1. Does «یطهّركم» (He purifies you) not mean that the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام were previously polluted and Allah purified them?

Answer

Purification is not conditional upon prior pollution, because the Quran uses the word «مطهّرة» (purified) regarding the Houris of Paradise, even though they have no pollution whatsoever. In other words, «یطهّركم» means keeping them pure, not cleansing them from previous pollution.

  1. If the Ahl al-Bayt are only five individuals, why is this Ayah placed among sentences related to the Prophet's wives?

Answer

A: The sentence «انّما یرید اللّه ...» was revealed separately but was placed here when the Quran was compiled.

B: According to Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, it was the custom of eloquent speakers, poets, and Arab literati to introduce a new and important topic amidst ordinary content so that its impact would be greater. Just as in the news broadcast, if the announcer suddenly says: Please pay attention to the news that has just reached me, this manner of speaking creates a psychological and promotional shock.

We read a similar instance in the third Ayah of Surah Al-Ma'idah, where the matter related to Wilayah (Guardianship), Imamate, the perfection of religion, the completion of favor, the despair of the disbelievers, and divine satisfaction, was revealed alongside rulings related to pork and blood. Such placements are the secret to preserving the Quran from the tampering of the unworthy. It is just like how women sometimes place their jewelry inside the cotton of a pillow when leaving the house. Although gold and cotton have no relation, this is done to protect them from the tampering of the unworthy.

C: Amidst the recommendations made to the Prophet's wives, He suddenly says: God has a special Will regarding the infallibility (Ismah) of the Ahl al-Bayt. Meaning, O wives of the Prophet! You are in a house where the Infallibles علیهم السلام reside, and you must certainly observe piety more strictly.

D: Regarding the Ahl al-Bayt, the masculine plural pronoun is used, indicating the predominance of males, contrary to the sentences related to the Prophet's wives, where the feminine plural pronoun is used. «بیوتكنّ, اقمن - عنكم, یطهّركم»

Although this Ayah addresses the Prophet's wives, the intent is certainly all Muslim women, who must act upon these commands.

390) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 391) Regarding contemporary Ignorance, a book titled "Jahiliyyah of the Twentieth Century" by Sayyid Qutb and another book titled "Portrait of Western Civilization" by Sayyid Mujtaba Musavi Lari have been written, which have been translated into several languages so far. 392) Tafsir Burhan. 393) Tafsirs Nur al-Thaqalayn and Namuneh. 394) At-Tahrim, 4.

Messages

  1. The proper place for a woman is either the home or outside the home without showing off. «قرن - لا تبرّجن»
  2. Regression and reverting to the era of Ignorance is forbidden. «لا تبرّجن تبرّج الجاهلیّته الاولى»
  3. Showing off is not a sign of civilization; it is a sign of Ignorance. «تبرّج الجاهلیّته الاولى»
  4. Prayer (Salat) and Zakat are two inseparable and interdependent elements. «الصلاة و... الزكوة»
  5. Obedience to the Messenger is obligatory alongside obedience to Allah. «اَطِعن اللّه »
  6. Sin is the spiritual filth (Rijs), and the Household of Prophethood must be far removed from this filth. «لیذهب عنكم الرّجس اهل البیت»

Ayah

وَٱذْكُرْنَ مَا يُتْلَىٰ فِى بُيُوتِكُنَّ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلْحِكْمَةِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ لَطِيفًا خَبِيرًا (۳٤)
And remember what is recited in your homes of the signs of Allah and wisdom. Indeed Allah is all-attentive, all-aware. (34)

Key Points

Two meanings have been stated for the word dhikr (remembrance): one is keeping in mind, and the other is uttering (bringing to the tongue). Therefore, wadhkurna (واذكرن) either means: O wives of the Prophet! Keep in mind and do not forget that Divine Revelation descends in your house, or it means that what is recited from the Book of Allah in your house and what you learn, you should convey to others.

Messages

1- The recitation of the Quran at home and its repetition by the family members is a recommendation of the Quran. «واذكرن ما یتلى... من آیات اللّه»

2- Instead of a culture of vulgarity, follow the Divine culture. «لا تبرّجن ... واذكرن»

3- The woman staying in the house and refraining from displaying herself does not mean falling behind in knowledge and wisdom. «قرن... لا تبرّجن... و اذكرن... آیات اللّه و الحكمة»

4- The family of prophethood is more deserving of performing good deeds «فى بیوتكنّ» (living in the house of revelation brings responsibility).

5- The establishment of the woman in the home, the prohibition of self-display, the performance of prayer and Zakat, and the remembrance of the verses, stem from Allah’s kindness toward women and His awareness of benefits and harms. «لطیفاً خبیرا»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَٱلْمُسْلِمَـٰتِ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ وَٱلْقَـٰنِتِينَ وَٱلْقَـٰنِتَـٰتِ وَٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ وَٱلصَّـٰدِقَـٰتِ وَٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ وَٱلصَّـٰبِرَٰتِ وَٱلْخَـٰشِعِينَ وَٱلْخَـٰشِعَـٰتِ وَٱلْمُتَصَدِّقِينَ وَٱلْمُتَصَدِّقَـٰتِ وَٱلصَّـٰٓئِمِينَ وَٱلصَّـٰٓئِمَـٰتِ وَٱلْحَـٰفِظِينَ فُرُوجَهُمْ وَٱلْحَـٰفِظَـٰتِ وَٱلذَّٰكِرِينَ ٱللَّهَ كَثِيرًا وَٱلذَّٰكِرَٰتِ أَعَدَّ ٱللَّهُ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةً وَأَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (۳٥)
Indeed the muslim men and the muslim women, the faithful men and the faithful women, the obedient men and the obedient women, the truthful men and the truthful women, the patient men and the patient women, the humble men and the humble women, the charitable men and the charitable women, the men who fast and the women who fast, the men who guard their private parts and the women who guard, the men who remember Allah greatly and the women who remember [Allah greatly]—Allah holds in store for them forgiveness and a great reward. (35)

Key Points

Following the instructions given to the wives of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, this Ayah enumerates ten perfections for men and women generally, covering matters of belief, ethics, and practice. The value of this Ayah becomes clear when we consider the discriminations that Arabs and non-Arabs (Ajam) imposed between men and women, and when we study the heartbreaking history of women. The difference between man and woman lies in the body. The soul is neither male nor female, and all human perfections relate to the soul, not the body. Therefore, all the perfections mentioned in this Ayah are presented equally for both men and women.

Messages

1- A human being becomes complete only when they possess all perfections, not just some of them. «انّ المسلمین ...»

2- A sign of the comprehensiveness of Islam is that it addresses perfection in all dimensions of human life: belief, ethics, practice, individual, and social. «انّ المسلمین ...»

3- By honoring the righteous, we encourage others to perform good deeds. «انّ المسلمین ...»

4- Human growth is gradual and step-by-step. (First, submission/Islam by the tongue, then faith/Iman by the heart, then obedience, truthfulness, patience, and so on.) «انّ المسلمین ... المؤمنین ... الصابرین ...»

5- The complete human being, in addition to verbal affirmation (Islam) and heartfelt belief (Iman), must control their stomach, desires, and tongue.

  • Control of the stomach through fasting. «الصائمین»

  • Control of the tongue through truthfulness. «الصادقین»

  • Control of desires by avoiding the forbidden (Haram). «والحافظین فروجهم»

  • Combating arrogance through humility. «القانتین »

  • Eliminating heedlessness through remembrance (Dhikr) and recollection of Allah. «والذاكرین»

  • Resistance against hardships and temptations through the power of patience (Sabr). «والصابرین»

  • Not neglecting the deprived through charity (Infaq). «والمتصدّقین»

6- Islam pays attention to both quantity and quality. (Regarding quantity, He says: «والذاكرین اللّه كثیرا» (those who remember Allah much), and regarding quality, He says: « والقانتین» (the obedient), which is obedience accompanied by humility.)

7- Women have the right to ownership and financial charity. «المتصدّقین والمتصدّقات»

8- We should attribute all our successes to Allah. (The remembrance of Allah is mentioned at the end of the perfections.) «و الذاكرین اللّه كثیراً»

9- Men and women are equal in the growth of spiritual and human perfections and in receiving reward. «انّ المسلمین والمسلمات... اعدّ اللّه لهم»

10- Good deeds are the key to Divine forgiveness. «اعدّ اللّه لهم مغفرة»

11- First Divine forgiveness, then the reception of the Divine reward. «مغفرة واجراً عظیما»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ وَلَا مُؤْمِنَةٍ إِذَا قَضَى ٱللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُۥٓ أَمْرًا أَن يَكُونَ لَهُمُ ٱلْخِيَرَةُ مِنْ أَمْرِهِمْ ۗ وَمَن يَعْصِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلَـٰلًا مُّبِينًا (۳٦)
A faithful man or woman may not have any option in their matter, when Allah and His Apostle have decided on a matter, and whoever disobeys Allah and His Apostle has certainly strayed into manifest error. (36)

Key Points

If Muslims had submitted to this Ayah, after the Holy Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) introduced Ali (عليه السلام) as his successor and the leader of the Islamic Ummah (community) by the command of Allah at Ghadir Khumm, saying: «من كنت مولاه فهذا علىّ مولاه» (Whoever I am his Mawla, then Ali is his Mawla), they would not have preferred anyone else over Ali (عليه السلام). This Ayah prepares the ground for the next Ayah, which tells the Prophet: You must break the wrong traditions, and people have no right to impose their personal preferences against the will of Allah. The fact that the noble Ayah states that man has no choice in the face of the command of God and His Messenger should not be surprising to anyone, because this is exactly like a doctor firmly telling his patient: You have no right to consume food or medicine other than what I have prescribed. Allah has also forbidden us from making free choices based on personal desire in opposition to Divine commands, because He knows the path to our happiness better than we do. We read in a narration that the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله علیه وآله) said: «یا عباد اللّه انتم كالمرضى و ربّ العالمین كالطّبیب ... اَلا فسلّموا للّه امره تكونوا من الفائزین» (O servants of Allah! You are like the sick, and the Lord of the Worlds is like the physician... Behold! Submit to the command of Allah so that you may be among the successful.) (395) O servants of God! You are like the sick, and God is like the physician. The patient's welfare lies in the physician's prescription, not in what the patient desires. Therefore, submit to the command of Allah so that you may attain salvation. In the system of religious governance, God-centricity prevails over democracy and popular sovereignty, because the opinion of the people is only valuable as long as it does not contradict the ruling of Allah.

395) Mizan al-Hikmah, Vol. 4, p. 545.

Messages

1- Age, literacy, race, power, wealth, fame, tribe, supporters, or international organizations—none of these create a right against Divine law. «ما كان لمؤمن ولا مؤمنة»

2- The sign of faith is devotion and submission (ta'abbud) to Allah and His Messenger. «ما كان لمؤمن ولا مؤمنة...»

3- There is no difference between men and women in submission and devotion. «لمؤمن ولا مؤمنة»

4- Whoever introduces a law against the law of Allah and the tradition (Sunnah) of the Prophet should doubt his own faith. «ما كان لمؤمن ولا مؤمنة»

5- The commands of the Holy Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) are mandatory to execute, just like the commands of Allah. «اذا قضى اللّه و رسوله»

6- Allah and His Messenger have complete authority (Wilayah) over the affairs of the people. «قضى اللّه و رسوله امراً»

7- Ijtihad (independent reasoning) in the face of a clear text (Nass) is forbidden. (Where the Divine ruling is clear, no one has the right to express an opinion.) «ما كان - لهم الخیرة»

8- Human freedom is within the framework of Divine laws. «ما كان - لهم الخیرة»

9- Reason must follow revelation (Wahy), and personal preference must follow duty. «ما كان - لهم الخیرة»

10- Any choice or theory that contradicts the law and judgment of Allah and His Messenger is manifest disobedience and deviation. «و من یعص اللّه ... ضلالاً مبینا»

Ayah

وَإِذْ تَقُولُ لِلَّذِىٓ أَنْعَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَأَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ وَٱتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ وَتُخْفِى فِى نَفْسِكَ مَا ٱللَّهُ مُبْدِيهِ وَتَخْشَى ٱلنَّاسَ وَٱللَّهُ أَحَقُّ أَن تَخْشَىٰهُ ۖ فَلَمَّا قَضَىٰ زَيْدٌ مِّنْهَا وَطَرًا زَوَّجْنَـٰكَهَا لِكَىْ لَا يَكُونَ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ حَرَجٌ فِىٓ أَزْوَٰجِ أَدْعِيَآئِهِمْ إِذَا قَضَوْا۟ مِنْهُنَّ وَطَرًا ۚ وَكَانَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ مَفْعُولًا (۳۷)
When you said to him whom Allah had blessed, and whom you [too] had blessed, ‘Retain your wife for yourself, and be wary of Allah,’ and you had hidden in your heart what Allah was to divulge, and you feared the people though Allah is worthier that you should fear Him, so when Zayd had got through with her, We wedded her to you, so that there may be no blame on the faithful in respect of the wives of their adopted sons, when the latter have got through with them, and Allah’s command is bound to be fulfilled. (37)

Key Points

Ad‘iyā’ (أدعياء) is the plural of da‘ī (دعى), meaning adopted son. Waṭar (وَطَر) means need or necessity. According to most commentators and historians, this Ayah relates to Zayd ibn Harithah. He was a slave whom Khadijah gifted to the Prophet of Islam, and the Prophet freed him. Zayd’s tribe had rejected him, and the Prophet adopted Zayd as His son. After the advent of Islam, Zayd embraced the religion of Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله, became one of the distinguished Muslims, served as the commander of the army in the Battle of Mu’tah, and was martyred in that same battle.

The Prophet proposed marriage to His cousin, Zaynab—who was a prominent woman and a granddaughter of ‘Abdul-Muṭṭalib—on behalf of Zayd. Initially, Zaynab thought the Prophet was proposing for Himself. However, when she realized it was for Zayd, she became upset, because until that time, no free woman (let alone prominent women) would marry a slave (even if he was a freed slave, the adopted son of the Prophet, and a distinguished Muslim).

After the marriage, incompatibility began between them, and Zayd decided to divorce Zaynab. He went to the Prophet repeatedly, but the Prophet kept telling him: Keep your wife and do not divorce her, «امسك علیك زوجك» (Keep your wife). In any case, Zayd divorced her, and Zaynab suffered two spiritual and social blows: first, marrying Zayd, which the society of that time disapproved of, and second, being divorced.

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was commanded by Allah to break two customs of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). A: Marrying the wife of an adopted son, which was considered unlawful in Jahiliyyah, just like marrying the wife of one's biological son. B: Marrying the wife of one's own slave, which was also considered ugly and unlawful at that time.

The Noble Prophet married Zaynab and provided a feast for the people that He did not provide for any of His other wives. Since the phrase «زوّجناكها» (We married her to you) appears in this Ayah, meaning We married Zaynab to you so that We might break the customs and traditions of Jahiliyyah through your action; Zaynab also boasted over the Prophet’s other wives, saying that their marriages were by the request of the Noble Prophet, but her marriage was by the command of Almighty Allah.

Question: What is the meaning of the phrase «تُخفى فى نفسك مَا اللّه مُبدیه» (you were concealing in your heart that which Allah was going to reveal), which Allah addresses to the Noble Prophet, saying: You were concealing an issue in your heart that Allah would disclose?

Answer: Imam Riḍā عليه السلام, in response to Ma’mūn’s questions regarding the infallibility of the Prophets, stated: Allah had informed the Prophet of the names of His future wives, and among them was the name of Zaynab bint Jaḥsh. The Prophet saw that she was Zayd’s wife, and wondered how Zayd’s wife would become His wife? This meaning was in the Prophet’s heart, and its wisdom was revealed through Allah’s decree concerning the Prophet’s marriage to Zaynab. (396)

Therefore, the Prophet never committed an act contrary to infallibility (Ismah) or the station of prophethood, and He had no prior interest in any woman, let alone the wife of Zayd, His adopted son! We seek refuge in Allah.

Commands are of two types: Mawlawī (obligatory), which must be acted upon, and Irshādī (advisory), acting upon which is beneficial to the person. Here, the command of the Noble Prophet, when He told Zayd: «امسك علیك زوجك» (Keep your wife), was advisory. Otherwise, a person whom Allah praised with the phrase «انعم اللّه علیه» (Allah bestowed favor upon him) would not have acted contrary to the Prophet’s command.

We also read in the previous Ayah: «و ما كان لمؤمن ولا مؤمنة اذا قضى اللّه و رسوله امرا ان یكون لهم الخیرة» (It is not for a believing man or a believing woman, when Allah and His Messenger have decreed a matter, to have any choice in their affair).

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله had no intention or desire to marry Zaynab; hence, His advice to Zayd was to keep his wife, not to divorce her. «امسك علیك زوجك... زوّجناكها» (Keep your wife... We married her to you). Considering the two subsequent Ayahs, which state that true messengers fear no one except Allah, «ولا یخشون احد۳ الاّ اللّه» (and they do not fear anyone except Allah), the meaning of the Prophet’s fear in this Ayah, «و تخشى الناس» (and you feared the people), was the fear of the negative atmosphere that would damage His goals, because people might say: The Prophet married the wife of a slave or the wife of His adopted son.

396) Tafāsīr al-Mīzān and Nūr al-Thaqalayn; ‘Uyūn al-Akhbār, Vol. 1, p. 281.

Messages

  1. Prophets were the authority for addressing people's issues. (Zayd ibn Harithah repeatedly informed the Prophet of his intention to divorce his wife, but the Prophet always told him: Be patient.) «و اذ تقول»
  2. Allah and the Messenger are our benefactors. «انعم اللّه - انعمت»
  3. When enumerating blessings, mention spiritual and divine blessings first, then material and worldly blessings. «انعم اللّه علیه» (referring to Zayd’s guidance and acceptance of Islam), «انعمت علیه» (referring to freeing Zayd from slavery).
  4. Islam and freedom are two great blessings. «انعم اللّه علیه» (the success of becoming Muslim); «انعمت علیه» (freeing the slave).
  5. If you wish to mention a characteristic of someone instead of their name, mention their best characteristics. «لِلّذى انعم اللّه علیه و انعمت علیه»
  6. Whenever a spouse complains to you, do not immediately suggest separation. «امسك علیك زوجك» (Islam supports strengthening the foundation of the family.)
  7. In enjoining good, use the leverage of emotion and piety. «امسك علیك زوجك اتّق اللّه»
  8. Piety (Taqwā) leads to the strengthening of family ties. «امسك علیك زوجك و اتّق اللّه»
  9. Being a benefactor creates the right to command and forbid. «انعمت علیه امسك علیك زوجك»
  10. The requirement of decisiveness and firmness is that we combat corrupt and deviant ideas, even if they are public opinion. «و تخشى الناس واللّه احقّ ان تخشاهُ»
  11. Do not fear the clamor of the people when breaking wrong social customs. «تخشى النّاس و اللّه احقّ ان تخشاه»
  12. In executing Allah’s command, do not wait for the approval of international bodies. «تخشى النّاس واللّه احقّ ان تخشاهُ»
  13. Not all divorces and separations are due to the woman being rebellious or disobedient (nāshizah). (A woman's lack of affection for her husband should not be a reason for her refusal to submit.) «قضى زیدٌ منها وطراً»
  14. Marital relations are among the important needs of humans. Waṭar (وطراً) means an important and fundamental need.
  15. Express marital issues euphemistically. «قضى زیدٌ منها وطراً»
  16. Religious leaders of the community must take the lead in breaking false traditions. «لكى لایكون على المؤمنین حرج» (Paving the way of truth for others is a virtue.)
  17. Widowed women are respectable, and marrying them is not a disgrace. «زوّجناكها» Allah says: We decreed this marriage.
  18. Adherence to non-divine customs and traditions paralyzes life. «لكى لا یكون على المؤمنین حَرَج»
  19. Marriage to the divorced wife of an adopted son is permissible. «اذا قضوا منهنّ وطراً» (Adoption existed in Islam.)

Ayah

مَّا كَانَ عَلَى ٱلنَّبِىِّ مِنْ حَرَجٍ فِيمَا فَرَضَ ٱللَّهُ لَهُۥ ۖ سُنَّةَ ٱللَّهِ فِى ٱلَّذِينَ خَلَوْا۟ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ وَكَانَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ قَدَرًا مَّقْدُورًا (۳۸)
