There is no blame on the Prophet in respect of that which Allah has made lawful for him: Allah’s precedent with those who passed away earlier (and Allah’s commands are ordained by a precise ordaining), (38)

Key Points

| This Ayah serves as a resolution to the previous Ayah, emphasizing that the religious leader must be a pioneer (line-breaker), must not fear blame, and must not wait for the satisfaction of others. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: "Since Allah knew that the hypocrites would use the Prophet's marriage to Zayd's wife—who was the adopted son of the حضرت (Prophet)—as a pretext for criticizing Him, He revealed this Ayah." (397)

397) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Uyun al-Akhbar, Vol. 1, p. 203.

Messages

1- The Prophet should not pay attention to the atmosphere creation or the obstacles when carrying out Allah's command. «ما كان على النبى ...»

2- The Prophet may also have specific duties and instructions. «فرض اللّه له»

3- All Prophets were pioneers and engaged in conflict with misguided public opinions. «من قبل»

4- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah involves breaking wrong traditions. «سنّة اللّه»

5- Divine commands are calculated (well-planned) and mandatory to execute. «امر اللّه قدراً مقدورا»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يُبَلِّغُونَ رِسَـٰلَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَخْشَوْنَهُۥ وَلَا يَخْشَوْنَ أَحَدًا إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ ۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ حَسِيبًا (۳۹)
such as deliver the messages of Allah and fear Him, and fear no one except Allah, and Allah suffices as reckoner. (39)

Key Points

This verse speaks about the decisiveness and courage of the preacher in conveying the divine commands to the people. However, we must know that in some cases, leniency, gentleness, and silence are necessary to attract hearts. Khashyah (reverential awe) is different from Khawf (fear). Khashyah is a heartfelt impression that arises from the greatness of a matter, whereas Khawf is the anticipation of an undesirable event which leads to avoiding it. The Prophets have no Khashyah of anyone other than Allah, because they see only Allah as Great.

Messages

1- Success in propagation has conditions:

A: Continuity of propagation. «یبلّغون»

B: Piety in action. «یخشونه»

C: Courage and decisiveness. «ولا یخشون احداً الا اللّه»

D: Reliance upon Allah. «و كفى باللّه حسیبا»

2- Propagation is not always through speech; sometimes the command of Allah must be clarified through action. (In the two preceding verses, He said: Marry Zayd's wife so that you may abolish a superstition. Then, in this verse, He says:) «الّذین یبلّغون رسالات اللّه»

3- Divine preachers always have staunch enemies and those who threaten them. «لایخشون احداً»

4- The awe of Allah is the prerequisite for bravery and courage. «یخشونه ولا یخشون احداً»

5- Divine preachers should know that the reckoning (reward) for their patience and endurance is with Allah. «كفى باللّه حسیبا»

Ayah

مَّا كَانَ مُحَمَّدٌ أَبَآ أَحَدٍ مِّن رِّجَالِكُمْ وَلَـٰكِن رَّسُولَ ٱللَّهِ وَخَاتَمَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمًا (٤٠)
Muhammad is not the father of any man among you, but he is the Apostle of Allah and the Seal of the Prophets, and Allah has knowledge of all things. (40)

Key Points

In this Ayah, both the word Rasūl (رسول) and the word Nabī (نبىّ) are mentioned. A Rasūl is the possessor of a Book, and a Nabī is the bearer of news, or alternatively, a Rasūl is the messenger, and a Nabī is the executor of the message. (398) Khātam (خاتَم) means the bezel of a ring, which in ancient times was used to seal letters and decrees by engraving one's name upon it. Khātam here is a metaphor for being the last Prophet.

Although there are numerous verses in the Quran indicating the universality and eternity of the religion of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله, such as: «للعالمین نذیرا» (399), «لانذركم به و من بلغ» (400), «كافّة للناس» (401), and in the narrations, the frequently transmitted (mutawātir) hadith «لا نبىّ بعدى» (There is no prophet after me) and the famous hadith «حلال محمّد حلال ابداً الى یوم القیامة» (The lawful of Muhammad is lawful forever until the Day of Resurrection) (402), and dozens of other hadiths have emphasized this point, this Ayah is the clearest proof of the Finality (Khātamiyyah) of the Prophet of Islam.

This Ayah is the only Ayah that mentions both the name of the Prophet and the prophethood of the Prophet in the form of two titles: "Muhammad," "Rasūl Allāh," "Khātam al-Nabiyyīn."

Several Questions

  1. Why do humans not need a new Prophet after Islam?

Answer: The main reason for the renewal of prophethoods and the arrival of new Prophets was two things: first, the distortion of the previous religion, such that many matters from the Book of God and the teachings of the previous Prophet were corrupted; and second, the evolution of humanity throughout history, which necessitated the revelation of more comprehensive and complete laws. However, after the Prophet of Islam, these two matters have not occurred. Firstly, because the Quran remains among the people without a single word of it being distorted, and secondly, the most comprehensive and complete laws have been presented within it, and based on Allah's knowledge, there is no need for humanity until the Day of Resurrection unless its ruling has been stated in Islam.

  1. How is the changing needs of humanity compatible with the Finality of the religion?

Answer: A person needs a teacher during their educational stages up to the level of Ijtihād (independent reasoning) or doctorate. After that, they reach a stage where they can derive their needs from what they have learned without a teacher. The door of Ijtihād is open, and necessary laws can be inferred from general principles. This is, of course, based on the principles that jurists (fuqahā) have established for extracting rulings.

  1. Why was humanity's connection with the unseen world severed?

Answer: Revelation (Wahy) is not descending, but the connection with the unseen remains. The infallible Imam عليه السلام is present, and divine angels descend upon him (403). Unseen assistance reaches worthy individuals; even ordinary people, if they are pious (muttaqī), acquire divine light, and Allah shows them the path of truth in some way. «اتّقوا اللّه... یجعل لكم نورا» (404)

398) Mu'jam al-Furūq al-Lughawiyyah. 399) Furqan, 1. 400) An'am, 19. 401) Saba', 28. 402) Kāfī, Vol. 1, p. 58. 403) Refer to Usūl al-Kāfī, discussion on Imamate. 404) Hadid, 28.

Messages

  1. An adopted son is never a son, nor is an adoptive father a father, such that his marriage to the divorced wife of his adopted son would be unlawful. «ما كان محمّد أبا أحد و رجالكم»

  2. The ruling regarding the Prophet's wives being mothers to the believers (which was discussed in Ayah 6) is not proof of the Prophet's fatherhood. «ما كان محمّد أبا أحد...»

  3. The Finality (Khātamiyyah) of the Prophet of Islam is based on divine knowledge and wisdom. «خاتم النبیّین - اللّه بكلّ شى ء علیما»

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ذِكْرًا كَثِيرًا (٤۱)
O you who have faith! Remember Allah with frequent remembrance, (41)
وَسَبِّحُوهُ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلًا (٤۲)
and glorify Him morning and evening. (42)

Key Points

The remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah is not only through the tongue and speech, but rather it is considering the satisfaction of Allah in every thought, action, and movement. The Quran has stated effects and blessings for the remembrance of Allah, and has considered one of the reasons for prayer (Salat) to be the remembrance of Allah: «اقم الصلاة لذكرى» (405) The remembrance of Allah is the only means for the tranquility of hearts. «الا بذكر اللّه تطمئن القلوب» (406) And the tranquil spirit and the satisfied soul take flight and reach the Truth. «یا ایّتها النّفس المطمئنّة . اِرجعى الى ربّك» (407) And turning away from the remembrance of Allah brings a miserable life. «و مَن اَعرض عن ذكرى فانّ له معیشة ضَنكا» (408) It has been said that the Tasbihat (glorifications) of Lady Fatimah al-Zahra عليها السلام is an example of abundant remembrance (Dhikr Kathir). (409) The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever possesses a remembering tongue (Lisan Dhakir) has been granted the good of this world and the Hereafter. And Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Everything has a limit except the remembrance of Allah. We read in a Hadith: The heart and soul rust like iron, and the remembrance of Allah is the means of illuminating and polishing it. It is mentioned in narrations: The remembrance of Allah is not only with the tongue; the true remembrance is that we remember Allah when facing the lawful (Halal) and the unlawful (Haram) and refrain from sin. (410)

405) Taha, 14. 406) Ra'd, 28. 407) Fajr, 27 - 28. 408) Taha, 124. 409) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 500. 410) Mizan al-Hikmah and Safinat al-Bihar (Dhikr).

Messages

  1. Beautiful and intimate address is a means of attracting people and preparing them for acceptance. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا»

  2. The remembrance of Allah is effective in a person only when it is continuous and abundant. «اُذكروا اللّه ذكراً كثیرا»

  3. The best remembrance of Allah is His glorification (Tasbih) and declaration of His transcendence (Tanzih). «اذكروا اللّه... و سبّحوه»

  4. We should spend the beginning and the end of the day with glorification and remembrance of Allah. «سبّحوه بُكرةً و اصیلاً»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُصَلِّى عَلَيْكُمْ وَمَلَـٰٓئِكَتُهُۥ لِيُخْرِجَكُم مِّنَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ إِلَى ٱلنُّورِ ۚ وَكَانَ بِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَحِيمًا (٤۳)
It is He who blesses you—and so do His angels—that He may bring you out from darkness into light, and He is most merciful to the faithful. (43)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, He said: Remember Allah often. In this Ayah, He says: Allah sends blessings (Salawat) upon you; it is as if Allah's blessings are your abundant remembrance, similar to the Ayah, "Remember Me, I will remember you." (411)

411) Al-Baqarah, 152.

Messages

1- The way to invite people to the religion is through blessings and mercy. «یُصلّى علیكم ... لیخرجكم»

2- The angels both send blessings upon the believers, «یصلّى علیكم و ملائكته لیخرجكم», and seek forgiveness for them. «و یستغفرون للذین آمنوا» (412)

3- In exiting the darknesses, man needs Divine assistance; human intellect and knowledge are not sufficient. «هو الّذى یصلّى ... لیخرجكم من الظلمات»

4- The darknesses are numerous. «الظلمات» The darkness of ignorance, polytheism (Shirk), division, heedlessness (Ghaflah), and superstitions.

5- Faith in Allah and the Divine truths is the light guiding man. «الى النور و كان بالمؤمنین»

6- Faith is the key to receiving special Divine mercy. «بالمؤمنین رحیماً»

412) Ghafir, 7.

Ayah

تَحِيَّتُهُمْ يَوْمَ يَلْقَوْنَهُۥ سَلَـٰمٌ ۚ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ أَجْرًا كَرِيمًا (٤٤)
The day they encounter Him, their greeting will be, ‘Peace,’ and He holds in store for them a noble reward. (44)

Key Points

The word tahiyyah (تحیّت) is derived from hayat (حیات), meaning to pray for the health and life of others. The intent of "the Day they meet Him" (یوم یلقونه), the "Day of Meeting," is the Day of Resurrection, when, with the removal of the veils, man attains the station of inner witnessing and heartfelt vision, as if he is meeting Allah.(413) Salam (peace) is both from Allah. "Peace—a word from a Merciful Lord." (سلام قولاً من ربّ رحیم)(414) And it is from the angels. "Peace be upon you for what you patiently endured." (سلام علیكم بما صبرتم)(415) And it is a sign of safety. "Enter it in peace, secure." (ادخلوها بسلام آمنین)(416) And it is the formal greeting of the people of Paradise. "A word: 'Peace, peace!'" (قیلاً سلاماً سلاماً)(417)

413) Tafsir-e Nemooneh. 414) Ya Sin, 58. 415) Ar-Ra‘d, 24. 416) Al-Hijr, 46. 417) Al-Waqi‘ah, 26.

Messages

1- Salam (peace) is the heavenly greeting and the greeting of the people of Paradise. "Their greeting... is Peace." (تحیّتهم... سلام)

2- Divine rewards are both noble and great and valuable. "A noble reward" (اجراً كریماً), "A great reward" (اجراً عظیماً)(418) 418) An-Nisa, 40.

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ شَـٰهِدًا وَمُبَشِّرًا وَنَذِيرًا (٤٥)
O Prophet! Indeed We have sent you as a witness, as a bearer of good news and as a warner (45)
وَدَاعِيًا إِلَى ٱللَّهِ بِإِذْنِهِۦ وَسِرَاجًا مُّنِيرًا (٤٦)
and as a summoner to Allah by His permission, and as a radiant lamp. (46)

Key Points

In these two verses, the role of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in society is explained. He invites people toward Allah, and His method in this invitation is glad tidings and warning, but not only through language; rather, His action is also a proof upon the people and a model for them.

Messages

1- The role and responsibility of individuals must be announced to them and to society. «یا ایّها النبى انّا ارسلناك...»

2- In the face of the enemy's stings and harassment, glorification is necessary. «ارسلناك شاهداً و...» (In the next verse, we read: «وَدَعْ اَذاهم»)

3- The Prophet is a witness over the deeds of the people. «شاهداً»

4- Only one who is a witness to the deeds and conduct of the people and is present on the scene can give glad tidings and warning. «شاهداً و مبشّراً»

5- In propagation and education, both glad tidings and warning are necessary. «مبشّراً و نذیرا»

6- Glad tidings and warning are the means of the Prophets' invitation. «مبشّرا و نذیرا و داعیاً»

7- The Prophets invite people toward Allah, not toward themselves. «داعیاً الى اللّه»

8- The Prophets also have no independence before Allah, and their success and invitation are by His permission. «باذنه»

9- The Prophet is a lamp of guidance in society, whose light causes growth, movement, and discernment. «سراجاً منیرا»

Ayahs

وَبَشِّرِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ بِأَنَّ لَهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ فَضْلًا كَبِيرًا (٤۷)
Announce to the faithful the good news that there will be for them a great grace from Allah. (47)
وَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ وَٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ وَدَعْ أَذَىٰهُمْ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ وَكِيلًا (٤۸)
Do not obey the faithless and the hypocrites, and disregard their torments, and put your trust in Allah, and Allah suffices as trustee. (48)

Key Points

Ignoring the harassment and annoyance of enemies does not mean weakness or negligence in dealing with them. Rather, it means reliance (Tawakkul) and hope in Divine Mercy, and not being discouraged by their actions. The Prophet is commanded by Allah to give glad tidings to the believers that His special grace and favor will encompass them, and this itself is the greatest favor from God. This Ayah, «و بشّر ...» (and give glad tidings...), is an instance and example of the previous Ayah which stated: «مبشّراً» (a bearer of glad tidings).

Messages

1- Faith is a prerequisite for receiving special Divine favors. «بشّر المؤمنین»

2- The leader must give glad tidings to the believers. «بشّر المؤمنین»

3- Allah's treatment of the believers is based on favor (فضل). «من اللّه فضلاً» (from Allah a favor). Of course, Divine favors upon servants have degrees and ranks. «فضلاً كبیراً» (a great favor).

4- Whoever is reassured by the source of true power can separate himself from the deviants. «من اللّه فضلا كبیرا - ولا تطع ...»

5- Ignoring the obstructionism of the disbelievers and hypocrites and resisting their demands requires reliance (Tawakkul). «لا تطع - دَع اَذاهم - توكّل»

6- The disbelievers and the hypocrites are on the same path. «ولا تطع الكافرین و المنافقین» (And obey not the disbelievers and the hypocrites). The work of both is to annoy the believers. «اذاهم» (their annoyance).

7- In certain circumstances, the leader must only guide and should not think of retaliation or revenge against the opponents. «دَع اَذاهم»

8- Let us rely upon Allah, for He is a worthy and sufficient Trustee (Wakil). «و كفى باللّه وكیلا»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا نَكَحْتُمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ ثُمَّ طَلَّقْتُمُوهُنَّ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَمَسُّوهُنَّ فَمَا لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ عِدَّةٍ تَعْتَدُّونَهَا ۖ فَمَتِّعُوهُنَّ وَسَرِّحُوهُنَّ سَرَاحًا جَمِيلًا (٤۹)
O you who have faith! When you marry faithful women and then divorce them before you touch them, there shall be no period for you to reckon. But provide for them and release them in a graceful manner. (49)

Key Points

The meaning of nikah (نكاح) is the marriage contract, the meaning of tamas (تماس) is sexual intercourse, and the meaning of sarāhan jamīlan (سراحاً جمیلاً) is divorce without hostility or violence. ‘Iddah (عِدّة) refers to the period during which women, after divorce, must wait until its end and not marry another husband. The ‘iddah for a woman’s divorce is three cycles of menstruation and purification, and the ‘iddah following the death of her husband is four months and ten days. Several groups of women do not require ‘iddah: one group consists of those who have not had sexual contact after the marriage contract; the other group consists of menopausal women who are despairing of having children (women aged 50 and 60). We also read in Ayah 236 of Surah Al-Baqarah that the gift given by the man to his divorced wife before intercourse must be according to what is customary and within his means: «على الموسع قدره و على المقتر قدره متاعاً بالمعروف». Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: These women (who are divorced) return to their father’s house with fear, grief, and the scorn of others. Therefore, you must soothe their hearts with a gift to the extent of your ability. «فمتّعوهن و سرّحوهنّ»

Messages

1- The prerequisite of faith is acting upon the commands of Allah. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا اذا نكحتم...»

2- The authority to divorce rests with the man. «طلّقتم» except in cases specified by the Shari‘ah (Islamic law).

3- The most important condition for marriage is faith. «آمنوا... نكحتم المؤمنات»

4- Divorce is not a sign of religious failure. «المؤمنات - طلّقتموهنّ»

5- The ‘iddah is the man’s right over the woman so that he may return (to the marriage) if he wishes. However, in a divorce before contact, the man has no right of return. «فما لكم علیهنّ من عدّة»

6- Emotional damage must be compensated. In a divorce following the marriage contract, the woman’s spirit is broken, and a portion of this is compensated by paying a suitable gift/settlement. «فمتّعوهنّ»

7- Separation and divorce do not mean hatred and violence. «سرّحوهنّ سراحاً جمیلاً»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ إِنَّآ أَحْلَلْنَا لَكَ أَزْوَٰجَكَ ٱلَّـٰتِىٓ ءَاتَيْتَ أُجُورَهُنَّ وَمَا مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ مِمَّآ أَفَآءَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ وَبَنَاتِ عَمِّكَ وَبَنَاتِ عَمَّـٰتِكَ وَبَنَاتِ خَالِكَ وَبَنَاتِ خَـٰلَـٰتِكَ ٱلَّـٰتِى هَاجَرْنَ مَعَكَ وَٱمْرَأَةً مُّؤْمِنَةً إِن وَهَبَتْ نَفْسَهَا لِلنَّبِىِّ إِنْ أَرَادَ ٱلنَّبِىُّ أَن يَسْتَنكِحَهَا خَالِصَةً لَّكَ مِن دُونِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۗ قَدْ عَلِمْنَا مَا فَرَضْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ فِىٓ أَزْوَٰجِهِمْ وَمَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُهُمْ لِكَيْلَا يَكُونَ عَلَيْكَ حَرَجٌ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (٥٠)
O Prophet! Indeed We have made lawful to you your wives whom you have given their dowries, and those whom your right hand owns, of those whom Allah gave you as spoils of war, and the daughters of your paternal uncle, and the daughters of your paternal aunts, and the daughters of your maternal uncle, and the daughters of your maternal aunts who migrated with you, and a faithful woman if she offers herself to the Prophet and the Prophet desires to take her in marriage (a privilege exclusively for you, not for [the rest of] the faithful; We know what We have made lawful for them with respect to their wives and those whom their right hands own, so that there may be no blame on you ), and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (50)

Key Points

“Afā'a” (أفاء) derived from “Fī'” (فَيْء) refers to wealth that reaches the Muslims without fighting. In Arabic, the words “’Amm” (paternal uncle) and “Khāl” (maternal uncle) are used generically and include all paternal and maternal uncles, but “’Ammah” (paternal aunt) and “Khālah” (maternal aunt) appear in the plural form: “’Ammātuk” (your paternal aunts), “Khālātuk” (your maternal aunts).

In Islam, there are three types of Nikah (marriage): A: Nikah with a dowry (Mahr), whether permanent or temporary. B: Nikah through the ownership of a slave woman (Kaniiz). C: Nikah without a dowry, where a woman grants herself to a man. The first and second types are for the general public, and the third type was exclusive to the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله.

One day, Aisha said: O Messenger of Allah! God favors you (or attends to your desires). The Prophet replied: «و انك ان اطعت اللّه سارع فى هواك» (419) And if you obey Allah, He will hasten to grant you favors.

Jihad (struggle) alone was obligatory upon the Prophet. «لا تكلّف الا نفسك و حرّض المؤمنین» (420) Night prayer (Salat al-Layl) was obligatory upon the Prophet, and... Now, one material privilege in exchange for all those hardships (421) is nothing.

Question: Why did the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله have multiple wives?

Answer: Firstly: The issue of lust was not the focus of the marriages of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, because the beloved Prophet of Islam lived only with Khadijah from the age of 25 until the age of 53—a woman who was fifteen years older than him when they married—and the Prophet did not marry anyone else while she was alive.

Secondly: All the tribes were against the Prophet, and the Prophet had to break this unholy unity with a precise plan. The best, simplest, cheapest, and most natural way was for the Prophet to become the son-in-law of various tribes. People, according to the customs of that time, considered it obligatory to support their son-in-law, and the Prophet could thus pave the way for propagating the religion.

Thirdly: Some marriages were intended to combat wrong customs and traditions, such as the Prophet’s marriage to Zaynab, his paternal aunt’s daughter, which was discussed in Ayah 37 of this very Surah.

Fourthly: The Prophet only contracted marriage (Aqd) with some women but did not consummate the marriage with them, and He only proposed to women from some tribes without even contracting the marriage. These actions served as a means of communication.

Fifthly: If the goal was sexual matters, He should have had many children from all these wives.

Sixthly: Aisha was very young when she was contracted in marriage to the Prophet, and a long time passed before she could become His actual wife. If the goal were sexual matters, a person would not marry a child.

Seventhly: We should not compare the Prophet with ourselves. We are hindered from many successes by having just one wife, but the Holy Prophet, despite having several wives, was so immersed in mysticism (Irfan), supplication (Munajat), and propagation that Allah addresses Him: Why are you so anxious for the guidance of the people «لعلّك باخع نفسك» (422) Why do you put yourself into hardship, «لتشقى» (423) Why do you forbid yourself things that are lawful, «لم تُحرّم ...» Yes, the strong breeze of Divine knowledge elevated the tent of the Prophet’s existence so high that all these women could not prevent it from soaring.

In any case, the Prophet’s subsequent marriages occurred after the age of 53, and they were with widows, abandoned women, or bereaved women, with their full willingness to marry, and with the Prophet maintaining justice, teaching a practical lesson on managing multiple wives, choosing a simple life, and disregarding the extravagant demands of some wives. Furthermore, having multiple wives was not considered a fault at that time, and sometimes the first wife would even propose marriage for the second wife, along with dozens of other points that must be considered.

419) Bihar, Vol. 22, p. 180. 420) Surah An-Nisa, 84. 421) The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: No Prophet has been tormented as much as I have been tormented. «ما اوذى نبّى مثل ما اوذیت» 422) Surah Ash-Shu'ara, 3. 423) Surah Ta Ha, 2.

Messages

1- The determination of lawful (Halal) and unlawful (Haram) is in the hands of Allah. «انّا احللنا لك» (Even the Prophet is subject to Divine law in personal matters.)

2- Dowry (Mahr) is necessary for the woman, and its payment by the man is obligatory. «آتیت اجورهن»

3- Take sexual instincts seriously; even slave women must be satisfied. «ما ملكت یمینك»

4- Islam has established specific duties as well as privileges for the person of the Prophet. «خالصة لك»

5- The determination of the women whom one may marry is from Allah. «فرضنا علیهم فى ازواجهم»

6- Allah does not wish the Prophet to be placed in hardship. «لكیلا یكون علیك حرج»

Ayah

۞ تُرْجِى مَن تَشَآءُ مِنْهُنَّ وَتُـْٔوِىٓ إِلَيْكَ مَن تَشَآءُ ۖ وَمَنِ ٱبْتَغَيْتَ مِمَّنْ عَزَلْتَ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ أَدْنَىٰٓ أَن تَقَرَّ أَعْيُنُهُنَّ وَلَا يَحْزَنَّ وَيَرْضَيْنَ بِمَآ ءَاتَيْتَهُنَّ كُلُّهُنَّ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى قُلُوبِكُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَلِيمًا (٥۱)
You may put off whichever of them you wish and consort with whichever of them you wish, and there is no sin upon you [in receiving again] any [of them] whom you may seek [to consort with] from among those you have set aside [earlier]. That makes it likelier that they will be comforted and not feel unhappy, and all of them will be pleased with what you give them. Allah knows what is in your hearts, and Allah is all-knowing, all-forbearing. (51)

Key Points

The word turjā (تُرجى) is derived from irjāʾ (ارجاء), meaning to postpone, and tuʾwī (تُؤى) is derived from maʾwā (مأوى), meaning to give shelter or place. These two words are a metaphor for divorce and retention, or for delaying and prioritizing cohabitation. Implementing justice among wives is obligatory, but the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, due to numerous difficulties, striving against enemies, and working for the community, was permitted by Allah to change the schedule of his wives. Instead of being in the house of one of them every night, he could stay in one place for several consecutive nights, and then in the house of another for several other nights.(424) Of course, some commentators have interpreted this verse regarding the Prophet's authority to increase or decrease the maintenance (nafaqah) of his wives, or to reject the request of the women who offered themselves (as a gift) and then accept them, or concerning initial divorce and subsequent return (rujūʿ).

(424) Derived from a hadith in Al-Kāfī, Vol. 5, p. 388.

Messages

1- Special authorities must be granted to individuals who bear heavy responsibilities. turjī man tashāʾ (ترجى من تشاء) (Those whose justice, piety, and performance are clear to everyone can make changes in their lives within the framework of justice and piety. Just as the wives of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله were confident in his just position and decision regarding the delay or prioritization of cohabitation with each of them.)

2- The Prophet's authorities do not mean disregarding the rights of his wives. taqarra aʿyunahunna - lā yaḥzanna - yarḍayna (تقرّ اعینهنّ - لا یحزنّ - یرضین)

3- A wife's eye is brightened by two things: first, the absence of grief, and second, inner satisfaction. taqarra aʿyunahunna wa lā yaḥzanna wa yarḍayna (تقرّ اعینهنّ و لایحزنّ و یرضینَ)

4- Satisfying the wife should not be limited to the first days of marriage; rather, it should always be so. wa yarḍayna (و یرضین)

5- Achieving one hundred percent satisfaction from multiple wives is ordinarily impossible, but planning must be done in such a way as to approach this goal. adnā an... yarḍayna (ادنى ان... یرضین)

6- A husband must strive to satisfy all of his wives. kullahunna (كلّهنّ)

7- Divine laws are for establishing justice and preserving family rights, even if the hearts desire something else. wa Allāhu yaʿlamu mā fī qulūbikum (واللّه یعلم ما فى قلوبكم)

8- Family laws in Islam originate from the source of Divine knowledge. kāna Allāhu ʿAlīman (كان اللّه علیماً)

Ayah

لَّا يَحِلُّ لَكَ ٱلنِّسَآءُ مِنۢ بَعْدُ وَلَآ أَن تَبَدَّلَ بِهِنَّ مِنْ أَزْوَٰجٍ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكَ حُسْنُهُنَّ إِلَّا مَا مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ رَّقِيبًا (٥۲)
Beyond that, women are not lawful for you, nor that you should change them for other wives even though their beauty should impress you, except those whom your right hand owns. Allah is watchful over all things. (52)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was under pressure from some tribes to marry a woman from among them so that they could boast that the Messenger of Allah was their son-in-law. The Prophet married some women based on certain expediencies, but this Ayah forbade the continuation of this practice.

Messages

1- In the Quran, there are fixed (permanent) and variable rulings. «لا یحلّ.. من بعد» (It is not lawful... after this). Yes, the element of time can be effective in the implementation of rulings.

2- Polygyny (multiple wives) is restricted even for the Prophet. «لا یحلّ.. من بعد»

3- Allah is watchful over those who replace their first wife with another woman merely because of her beauty. «رقیباً»

4- The Prophet is obligated to receive the lawful (halal) and unlawful (haram) aspects of his actions from Allah. «لا یحلّ لك»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَدْخُلُوا۟ بُيُوتَ ٱلنَّبِىِّ إِلَّآ أَن يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ إِلَىٰ طَعَامٍ غَيْرَ نَـٰظِرِينَ إِنَىٰهُ وَلَـٰكِنْ إِذَا دُعِيتُمْ فَٱدْخُلُوا۟ فَإِذَا طَعِمْتُمْ فَٱنتَشِرُوا۟ وَلَا مُسْتَـْٔنِسِينَ لِحَدِيثٍ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكُمْ كَانَ يُؤْذِى ٱلنَّبِىَّ فَيَسْتَحْىِۦ مِنكُمْ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَسْتَحْىِۦ مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ ۚ وَإِذَا سَأَلْتُمُوهُنَّ مَتَـٰعًا فَسْـَٔلُوهُنَّ مِن وَرَآءِ حِجَابٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ أَطْهَرُ لِقُلُوبِكُمْ وَقُلُوبِهِنَّ ۚ وَمَا كَانَ لَكُمْ أَن تُؤْذُوا۟ رَسُولَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَآ أَن تَنكِحُوٓا۟ أَزْوَٰجَهُۥ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦٓ أَبَدًا ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكُمْ كَانَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ عَظِيمًا (٥۳)
O you who have faith! Do not enter the Prophet’s houses for a meal until you are granted permission, without hanging around for it to be readied. But enter when you are invited, and disperse when you have taken your meal, without cozying up for chats. Such conduct on your part offends the Prophet, and he is ashamed of [asking] you [to leave]; but Allah is not ashamed of [expressing] the truth. When you ask [his] womenfolk for something, do so from behind a curtain. That is more chaste for your hearts and theirs. You should not offend the Apostle of Allah, nor may you ever marry his wives after him. Indeed that would be a grave [sin] with Allah. (53)

Key Points

Seeking permission to enter the homes of others is not exclusive to the house of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, just as we read in Surah An-Noor: «لا تدخلوا بیوتاً غیر بیوتكم حتّى تستأنسوا» (425)

In this Ayah, some aspects of the etiquette of hosting/visiting are discussed, and in other Ayahs, other parts of it are mentioned:

  • Upon entering, give salutations (Salam). «فسلّموا» (426)
  • If they refuse entry, do not be upset and return. «اذا قیل لكم ارجعوا فارجعوا» (427)
  • The manner of hospitality, especially if you have invited them, should be good. «و جاء بعجل حنیذ» (428)
  • Bring the food (tablecloth/spread) to the guest, rather than taking the guest to the place of hospitality. «فقرّبه الیهم» (429)

Dozens of points and instructions regarding this have also been mentioned in the narrations.

Ināh (اِناه) means the cooking/ripening of food, and ghayra nāẓirīna ināh (غیر ناظرین اناه) means that the guest should not arrive before the time of hospitality, so as to wait for the food to be cooked.

The Prophet’s house had multiple rooms, and each of the wives lived in a room. «بیوت النبىّ»

The addresses given in this Ayah concerning the wives of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله are, in reality, related to all Muslim women, due to the phrase «اطهر لقلوبكم و قلوبهنّ» (that is purer for your hearts and their hearts), as Allah desires purity of heart for every man and woman.

Since the danger of misusing the status of the Prophet’s wives is more important than their personal right, the right to marry others must be revoked from them after the Prophet’s passing. «ولا ان تنكحوا ازواجه»

425) An-Noor, 27. 426) An-Noor, 61. 427) An-Noor, 28. 428) Hud, 69. 429) Adh-Dhariyat, 27.

Messages

1- Faith necessitates observing social etiquette and manners. «یا ایها الّذین آمنوا لا تدخلوا...»

2- The right to peace and tranquility in one's dwelling is respected for everyone, especially for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله. «لا تدخلوا بیوت النبىّ»

3- It is better for hospitality to take place in the home (not in a guesthouse or hotel). «بیوت النبىّ... الى طعام»

4- The host has the right not to grant permission for entry to anyone. «الاّ ان یُؤذن لكم»

5- Permission to enter the house of others is sufficient in any form, and it is not necessary for the host to personally grant permission. (Yu’dhan (یؤذن) is in the passive form, meaning the host’s permission can be obtained through any means.)

6- Hosting guests is part of the conduct (Sīrah) of the Noble Prophet. «الى طعام... دُعیتم»

7- The principle of hospitality is important, not the type of food. «الى طعام... دُعیتم»

8- A comprehensive religion is one that has programs for both minor issues, such as hosting guests, and the greatest governmental issues. «یؤذن لكم - غیر ناظرین - فانتشروا ...»

9- Do not go to a gathering without an invitation. «اذا دعیتم»

10- In Islam, ordinary people can visit and even be guests in the house of the foremost religious personality. «بیوت النبىّ... دُعیتم»

11- It is part of the etiquette of hospitality to leave the house after eating the food. «فانتشروا» And do not make the host’s home a place for prolonged conversation. «و لامستأنسین لحدیث»

12- Harming the host is forbidden. «یُؤذى النبى» (Even if it is by bringing children or companions. Belittling the food and considering it insignificant is also an instance of harming the host.)

13- The Prophet suffered from the waste of time in unnecessary conversations. «لا مستأنسین لحدیث انّ ذلكم كان یُؤذى النّبى»

14- Offending does not have to be physical or apparent; moral and psychological pressure is also a form of harm. «ذلكم كان یُؤذى»

15- Do not put the host in a difficult position or under obligation (due to politeness/shyness). «فیستحى منكم»

16- Silence based on shyness or obligation is not a sign of consent. «فیستحى منكم»

17- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was a person of great modesty (Haya). «فیستحى منكم»

18- If a person remains silent due to shyness for some reason, others should break the silence, state the truth, and relieve him of the burden of embarrassment. «واللّه لا یستحى من الحق»

19- Communication and interaction (transactions) by women with others is permissible, provided Hijab is maintained. «فاسئلوهنّ من وراء حجاب»

20- The wives of the Prophet were not confined and were in communication with the outside world. (430) «فاسئلوهنّ من وراء حجاب»

21- When arguing for the necessity of a woman’s Hijab, present a reason that is agreed upon by all. «ذلكم اطهر لقلوبكم»

22- In the manner of interaction between men and women, the axis of actions must be piety (Taqwa) and purity of heart. «اطهر لقلوبكم و قلوبهنّ»

23- The gaze of non-mahrams (unrelated persons) upon one another affects their hearts. «فاسئلوهن من وراء حجاب ذلكم اطهر لقلوبكم و قلوبهنّ»

From the hand of both the eye and the heart, a cry! Whatever the eye sees, the heart remembers.

24- Hijab is beneficial for both men and women. «اطهر لقلوبكم و قلوبهنّ»

25- Interaction with a non-mahram without maintaining Hijab causes distress to the Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله. «و ما كان لكم ان تُؤذوا رسول اللّه»

26- Harming the Prophet is among the major sins. «كان عنداللّه عظیما»

(430) Perhaps today there are people who would be upset if you asked their wife for a glass of water, asking why you spoke to my wife, but the Quran states: while maintaining Hijab, one may request something even from the wives of the Prophet.

Ayah

إِن تُبْدُوا۟ شَيْـًٔا أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمًا (٥٤)
Whether you disclose anything or hide it, Allah indeed knows all things. (54)

Messages

1- Allah is aware not only of our deeds but of everything. «فانّ اللّه كان بكلّ شى ء علیما»

2- Allah's knowledge of hidden and manifest matters is equal. «كان بكلّ شى ء علیما»

3- If we know that everything is clear to Him, why engage in ostentation (riya) and hypocrisy (nifaq)? «بكلّ شى ء علیما»

4- Maintaining outward observance while establishing secret relationships is a sign of disbelief in Allah's knowledge. «تبدوا شیئاً او تخفوه فانّ اللّه... علیما»

Ayah

لَّا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِنَّ فِىٓ ءَابَآئِهِنَّ وَلَآ أَبْنَآئِهِنَّ وَلَآ إِخْوَٰنِهِنَّ وَلَآ أَبْنَآءِ إِخْوَٰنِهِنَّ وَلَآ أَبْنَآءِ أَخَوَٰتِهِنَّ وَلَا نِسَآئِهِنَّ وَلَا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُهُنَّ ۗ وَٱتَّقِينَ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدًا (٥٥)
There is no sin on them [in socializing freely] with their fathers, or their sons, or their brothers, or their brothers’ sons, or the sons of their sisters, or their own womenfolk, or what their right hands own. Be wary of Allah. Indeed Allah is witness to all things. (55)

Key Points

This verse names those who are mahram (unmarriageable kin) to a woman through lineage. However, mahrams through affinity, such as the son-in-law and foster brother, as well as children who have not reached the age of religious obligation and do not understand sexual matters, are also mahram to women. (431) Although some have considered this verse to relate to the wives of the Prophet, it seems that the verse relates to all women.

(431) Regarding the issues of hijab and mahram and namahram (non-mahram), refer to the Tawdih al-Masā'il (Clarification of Rulings) of the Marāji' al-Taqlīd (Sources of Emulation).

Messages

1- There is no hardship (ḥaraj) or difficulty in Islam. First-degree relatives who constantly frequent the family home are mahram to one another. «لا جناح ...»

2- Hijab is not necessary among Muslim women, but hijab is necessary in the presence of non-Muslim women. «نسائهنّ»

3- Being mahram does not mean carelessness or lack of piety. «لاجناح علیهنّ... واتّقین اللّه»

4- Allah being a Witness over human actions is the best factor and leverage for observing piety. «واتّقین اللّه انّ اللّه كان على كلّ شى ء شهیدا»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ وَمَلَـٰٓئِكَتَهُۥ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى ٱلنَّبِىِّ ۚ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ صَلُّوا۟ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا۟ تَسْلِيمًا (٥٦)
Indeed Allah and His angels bless the Prophet; O you who have faith! Invoke blessings on him and invoke Peace upon him in a worthy manner. (56)

Key Points

Narrations are cited in the six canonical books of the Ahl al-Sunnah (Sunni tradition) stating that the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was asked: How should we send blessings (Salawat)? He replied: Say: «اللّهم صلّ على محمّد و آل محمّد» (O Allah, bless Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad). When Sahih Bukhari (432) (Chapter on the Manner of Sending Blessings upon the Prophet) narrates this Hadith, it omits the phrase «آل محمّد» (Aal Muhammad) in that very narration, writing thus: Muhammad «صلّى اللّه علیه و سلّم» was asked: How should we send blessings (Salawat)? He replied: Say: «اللّهم صلّ على محمّد و على آل محمّد»! (O Allah, bless Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad)!?

Mentioning Salawat alongside the name of the Prophet is important. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: "Whoever sends Salawat upon me in a book or writing, as long as that Salawat remains in that writing, there will be a reward for him." (433)

We read in a Hadith: "Whoever sends one Salawat upon Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله, Allah sends ten Salawat upon him and covers ten of his errors." (434)

Salawat is the key to the acceptance of supplication (Du'a) and causes the scale of the believer to be heavy on the Day of Judgment.

Allah says to His Prophet in the Quran: Send blessings upon those who give Zakat (charity), «خذ من اموالهم صدقة... صلّ علیهم» (Take from their wealth a charity... and pray for them) (435). In this Ayah, He commands the people to send Salawat upon the Prophet.

Yes, in Islam, the relationship between the people and the leader is one of Salawat and greetings (Durud), just as in Ayah 54 of Surah Al-An'am, Allah instructs His Prophet to greet those who come to meet him: «اذا جاءك الّذین یؤمنون بآیاتنا فقل سلام علیكم» (When those who believe in Our signs come to you, say, "Peace be upon you").

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "The blessings (Durud) of Allah mean mercy, the blessings of the angels mean purification (or remembrance in purity), and the blessings of the people mean supplication (Du'a)." (436)

432) Sahih Bukhari, Hadith 5880. 433) Tafsir Ruh al-Bayan. 434) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 435) At-Tawbah, 103. 436) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Whenever you wish to invite others to a good deed, first begin with yourselves and those involved. (When commanding people to send Salawat, Allah first mentions His own Salawat and that of the angels.) «انّ اللّه و ملائكته یصلّون»

2- The Salawat of Allah and the angels is continuous. «یصلّون»

3- Sending Salawat upon the Prophet is a requirement of faith and one of the duties of the believers. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا صلّوا»

4- Verbal greetings (Durud) are not sufficient; practical submission (Taslim) is also necessary. «صلّوا علیه و سلّموا»

5- The relationship between the people and the leader in the divine government is one of Salawat and Salam (peace/greetings). (Heartfelt affection is not enough; expressing that affection is necessary.) «صلّوا علیه و سلّموا»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ لَعَنَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا مُّهِينًا (٥۷)
Indeed those who offend Allah and His Apostle are cursed by Allah in the world and the Hereafter, and He has prepared a humiliating punishment for them. (57)

Key Points

Harming Allah means performing an action contrary to His will and pleasure, which, instead of attracting Divine mercy, results in Allah's wrath and curse. Perhaps the intent behind harming Allah is harming the servants of Allah, similar to lending money to a servant, which is considered lending money to Allah. And the intent behind harming the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is denying Him, slandering Him, treating Him disrespectfully, and harming His Ahl al-Bayt (Household), just as it is mentioned in several narrations that the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «فاطمة بضعة منّى فمن اغضبها اغضبنى»(437) Fatimah is a part of me; whoever angers her has angered me.

437) Sahih Bukhari, Vol. 5, p. 26, and Sahih Muslim, Vol. 4, p. 1903.

Messages

1- A sincere servant reaches a status where annoying him becomes annoying Allah. «یؤذون اللّه و رسوله»

2- A portion of the punishment for insulting, humiliating, and harming the people of faith appears in this very world. «لعنهم اللّه فى الدنیا»

3- Since the goal of the one causing harm is humiliation, his punishment in the Hereafter is also humiliation. «عذاباً مهینا»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ بِغَيْرِ مَا ٱكْتَسَبُوا۟ فَقَدِ ٱحْتَمَلُوا۟ بُهْتَـٰنًا وَإِثْمًا مُّبِينًا (٥۸)
Those who offend faithful men and women undeservedly, certainly bear the guilt of slander and flagrant sin. (58)

Key Points

In cases of physical harm, Qisas (retaliation) and reciprocity are permissible: «والعین بالعین و الانف بالانف و الاُذن بالاُذن و السّن بالسّن»(438). However, regarding spiritual harm and damage to reputation, we do not have the command for reciprocity. The Quran does not say that if someone slanders you, you should slander them back. Rather, it warns the sinner, and if necessary, they receive the prescribed punishment (hadd) by the order of the religious judge (hakim shar'i). Perhaps one can infer from the phrase «بغیر ما اكتسبوا» (for what they have not earned) that harming a transgressing Muslim is permissible within the framework of Divine law.

438) Ma'idah, 45.

Messages

1- Persistence in sin increases its ugliness and punishment. («یُؤذون» [they harm] is a sign of continuity and insistence on causing harm.)

2- The reputation of believing men and women is sacred, and damaging it is a great sin. (Protecting one's reputation is not dependent on being male or female.) «المؤمنین والمؤمنات»

3- A sin that carries punishment is one committed knowingly and intentionally. «اكتسبوا»

4- Sin burdens a person heavily. «احتملوا»

5- The most significant harm is verbal abuse and slander. «یُؤذون - بهتاناً»

6- Harming and annoying others, in whatever form, is among the sins that incur Divine wrath. «یُؤذون... اثماً مبیناً»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ قُل لِّأَزْوَٰجِكَ وَبَنَاتِكَ وَنِسَآءِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يُدْنِينَ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِن جَلَـٰبِيبِهِنَّ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ أَدْنَىٰٓ أَن يُعْرَفْنَ فَلَا يُؤْذَيْنَ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (٥۹)
O Prophet! Tell your wives and your daughters and the women of the faithful to draw closely over themselves their chadors [when going out]. That makes it likely for them to be recognized and not be troubled, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (59)

Key Points

Jalābīb is the plural of jilbāb, meaning a veil (miqna'ah) that covers the head and neck, (439) or a long cloth that covers the entire body, head, and neck. (440) In the previous verses, the discussion was about the harassment and abuse of believers by the hypocrites. This verse states: In order that believing women are not subjected to harassment by lewd individuals, they must cover themselves in such a way that no opportunity for their dishonor is provided.

Based on this verse, a type of head covering was used by women at that time, but the women were not careful in covering themselves from non-mahram (unrelated men). Therefore, the recommendation of this verse is to draw the jilbāb close to themselves, not the jilbāb itself, which was already customary.

This verse, which commands the hijab, is not presented with a threat, but rather explains the philosophy of hijab as follows: Women and girls, if they wish to be safe from the taunts, accusations, assaults, and threats of corrupt and lewd individuals, must cover themselves.

Afflictions of Lack of Hijab 1. The flourishing of leering and lustfulness. 2. The expansion of corruption and immorality. 3. Assault and rape. 4. Illegitimate pregnancies and abortion. 5. The emergence of psychological and sexually transmitted diseases. 6. Suicide and running away from home due to loss of honor. 7. The coldness of leering men towards their own wives. 8. The rise in divorce rates and the weakening of family relations. 9. Competition in extravagance. 10. Creating anxiety for chaste families.

439) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 440) Tafsir Al-Mīzān.

Messages

  1. In enjoining good, we should start with our own people first, then others. «قل لازواجك و بناتك و نساء المؤمنین»
  2. In divine rulings, there is no difference between the wives and daughters of the Prophet and other women. «لازواجك و بناتك و نساء المؤمنین»
  3. Wearing clothing that identifies a woman's character for the sake of ensuring chastity and honor is necessary. «یعرفن فلا یؤذین»
  4. Do not give excuses to the sick-hearted and those who disrupt public chastity. (In the previous verses, the warning was directed at the harassers; in this verse, the duty of those being harassed is addressed.) «یعرفن فلا یؤذین»
  5. The movement of women outside the home is permissible, provided public chastity is maintained. «یعرفن فلا یؤذین»
  6. In enjoining good and forbidding evil, state the reason for your command or prohibition. «فلا یُؤذین»
  7. The type of clothing people wear in society can be an indicator of their character, school of thought, and goal. «یعرفن فلا یؤذین»
  8. The source of many harassments by lustful individuals towards women and girls is the type of clothing they themselves wear. «یعرفن فلا یُؤذین»
  9. Veiled women must, through their presence in the public sphere, maintain the general atmosphere of modesty and chastity and deny opportunities for maneuver to the lustful. «یعرفن فلا یؤذین»
  10. Past instances of improper hijab are forgivable. «غفوراً رحیماً»
  11. Allah’s forgiveness is accompanied by His mercy. «كان اللّه غفوراً رحیماً»

Ayah

۞ لَّئِن لَّمْ يَنتَهِ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ وَٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ وَٱلْمُرْجِفُونَ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ لَنُغْرِيَنَّكَ بِهِمْ ثُمَّ لَا يُجَاوِرُونَكَ فِيهَآ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (٦٠)
If the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness, and the rumourmongers in the city do not desist, We will prompt you [to take action] against them; then they will not be your neighbours in it except briefly. (60)

Key Points

Al-Murjifūn (مُرجفون), derived from Irjāf (اِرجاف), refers to those who create anxiety in the Islamic society through rumor-mongering. These were people who did not go to the front lines but spread rumors in the city that the Muslims had been defeated and the disbelievers had won, or that the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) had been martyred. By this vile action, they caused distress among the people.

Lanughriyannaka (لنُغرینّك), derived from Ighrā’ (اِغراء), means "to stick" or "to attach," and is a metaphor for comprehensive dominance/control.

If we view this Ayah as a continuation of the previous Ayah, the intent is that the hypocrites and those with sickness in their hearts in Medina were harassing believing women (which is why Allah commanded women to observe full covering in the previous Ayah), and in this Ayah, He warns these sick-hearted individuals. It is also possible that this Ayah is independent, and the intent is to confront and eradicate the conspirators who create anxiety through cold war tactics and rumor-mongering.

Although the severest threats are mentioned in this Ayah, and the Islamic society has always suffered the greatest blows from rumor-mongers, unfortunately, this topic has received little attention in books of jurisprudence (fiqh). Even one of the Mujtahids (jurists) who served as a judge for years said: "I searched 54 books of fiqh and did not find a chapter dedicated to the subject of Irjāf (اِرجاف)."

The final phrase of this Ayah, «لَنُغرِیَنَّكَ بِهِم ثُمَّ لَا یُجَاوِرُونَكَ ...», can be interpreted in two ways: A: We shall so incite you against them that they will all be eradicated and will not be able to remain near you except for a short period. B: We shall so scatter them that only a small number of them will remain in your vicinity.

Messages

1- Before confronting offenders, establish the conclusive argument (Itmām al-Hujjah). «لَئِن لَّم یَنتَهِ... لَنُغرِیَنَّكَ»

2- Patience and tolerance have limits. «لَئِن لَّم یَنتَهِ المُنافِقونَ» Leniency and compromise are not always effective.

3- Those with sickness in their hearts are alongside the hypocrites. «المُنافِقونَ - الَّذینَ فی قُلوبِهِم مَرَضٌ»

4- The uprising of Muslims is not based on revenge or desire, but by the Will and Command of Allah. «لَنُغرِیَنَّكَ»

5- The loyal Ummah (community) must be unified and integrated into the leader. «لَنُغرِیَنَّكَ» instead of lanughriyannakum (لنغرینّكم).

6- The first person who must rise up against those who endanger the security of society is the Islamic leader. «لَنُغرِیَنَّكَ بِهِم»

7- Islam attaches such importance to social security that it has issued the command to expel those who create anxiety and insecurity among the people. «لَنُغرِیَنَّكَ بِهِم ثُمَّ لَا یُجَاوِرُونَكَ»

8- Enemies must be intimidated by the uprising and revolutionary action of the Muslims. «لَنُغرِیَنَّكَ بِهِم ثُمَّ لَا یُجَاوِرُونَكَ»

Ayahs

مَّلْعُونِينَ ۖ أَيْنَمَا ثُقِفُوٓا۟ أُخِذُوا۟ وَقُتِّلُوا۟ تَقْتِيلًا (٦۱)
Accursed, they will be seized wherever they are confronted and slain violently: (61)
سُنَّةَ ٱللَّهِ فِى ٱلَّذِينَ خَلَوْا۟ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّةِ ٱللَّهِ تَبْدِيلًا (٦۲)
Allah’s precedent with those who passed away before, and you will never find any change in Allah’s precedent. (62)

Key Points

The word Thuqifū (ثُقِفوا) derives from Thiqāfah (ثِقافة), meaning precise and skillful attainment or mastery. Therefore, knowledge and culture are referred to as Thiqāfah. Some commentators believe all these strict measures apply to those who harass believers, as mentioned in the previous verses. But it seems that all these threats and the declaration of being mahdūr ad-dam (having their blood forfeit/lawful to kill) are intended for those who intend to disrupt the security of society and overthrow the system, and not merely those who harass people's honor.

Messages

  1. "Death to the hypocrite" is a Quranic slogan. «المنافقون... ملعونین»
  2. The execution of Allah's decree concerning seditionists who fight against the truth is not limited by time, place, or borders. «اینما ثقفوا اُخذوا»
  3. There should be no safe haven for seditionists and those who disrupt societal security. «اینما ثقفوا اُخذوا»
  4. The flight of seditionists should not prevent you from confronting them. «اینما ثقفوا اُخذوا»
  5. In apprehending enemies, all people must mobilize. «اینما ثقفوا اُخذوا»
  6. Those who disturb the security of the people have no security for their lives. «اُخذوا و قتّلوا»
  7. Sometimes, the sinister plot of the enemy, which takes the form of a cold war, must be answered with a hot war. «مرجفون - قُتّلوا»
  8. Those who do not cease creating sedition and anxiety must be killed severely and humiliatingly. «قتّلوا تقتیلا»
  9. Jihad against seditionists has been a Divine tradition (Sunnat Allah) in all religions. «سنّة اللّه»
  10. In the method of propagation, link severe and heavy rulings with their historical precedent. «كتب علیكم الصیام كما كتب على الّذین من قبلكم»(441), «و كایّن من نبّى قاتل معه...»(442), «قتّلوا تقتیلا سنّة اللّه ... من قبل»
  11. The status of humanity and the value of security are so high in all Divine religions that the severest punishments are applied to those who harm society through this means. «قتّلوا - سنّة اللّه ... من قبل»
  12. The law regarding the execution of seditionists and disruptors is unchangeable. «ولن تجد لسنّة اللّه تبدیلا»

Ayah

يَسْـَٔلُكَ ٱلنَّاسُ عَنِ ٱلسَّاعَةِ ۖ قُلْ إِنَّمَا عِلْمُهَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَا يُدْرِيكَ لَعَلَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ تَكُونُ قَرِيبًا (٦۳)
The people question you concerning the Hour. Say, ‘Its knowledge is only with Allah.’ What do you know, maybe the Hour is near. (63)

Key Points

The motivations for asking questions are numerous: * Sometimes the question is for testing. Like examination questions. * Sometimes the question is out of mockery. Like the questioning of an arrogant powerful person toward a weak one. * Sometimes the question is out of astonishment. Like the questioning of a fearful person toward a brave one. * Sometimes the question is for cornering (leading to a dead end). Like the questioning of an interrogator toward a criminal. * Sometimes the question is for misleading. Like the questioning of a deviant teacher toward a student. * Sometimes the question is for creating doubt in others. Like the questioning of deviants. * Sometimes the question is for relieving worry. The questioning of a mother toward her child. * Sometimes the question is for knowing. Like the questioning of the ignorant toward the knowledgeable. * Sometimes the question is for reprimand. Like the questioning of a teacher about the reason for a student's laziness. In this Ayah too, the question about the time of the Hour (Qiyamah) can have different motivations. The word qareeb (قریب) is used for both masculine and feminine. «لعلّ الساعة تكون قریبا» and He did not say: qareebah (قریبة), similar to the phrase «انّ رحمت اللّه قریب من المحسنین» (443). The end of the Ayah is a form of consolation for the Prophet, telling him not to be weary of the excuses of the disbelievers, as their reckoning will soon be addressed. «تكون قریبا»

443) Al-A'raf, 56.

Messages

  1. Although Allah informs the Prophets of the Unseen, «لا یظهر على غیبه احداً الاّ مَن ارتضى من رسول» (444), the knowledge of the Prophets is also limited. «علمها عند اللّه»
  2. It is neither necessary for us to know everything, nor is it necessary for us to answer every question. «علمها عند اللّه»
  3. Not knowing the time of the occurrence of the Hour does not harm the faith and certainty in its principle. «علمها عند اللّه»
  4. Some knowledge is exclusive to Allah. «علمها عند اللّه»
  5. Not knowing everything is not a defect. «و ما یدریك» (Rather, if we knew the time of the occurrence of the Hour, we would lose our balance.)
  6. In education (or upbringing), individuals must be kept between fear and hope. «لعلّ»
  7. We must be ready for the occurrence of the Hour at every moment. «لعلّ السّاعة تكون قریبا» 444) Al-Jinn, 26 - 27.

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَعَنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ سَعِيرًا (٦٤)
Indeed Allah has cursed the faithless and prepared for them a blaze, (64)
خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۖ لَّا يَجِدُونَ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا (٦٥)
in which they will remain forever, and will not find any guardian or helper. (65)

Messages

1- Allah's wrath towards the disbelievers is certain. The word «اَعدَّ» (He has prepared) indicates that Hell already exists.

2- The disbelievers will have neither a protector (Wali) who can save them from entering Hell nor a helper (Nasir) who can rescue them. «لا یجدون ولیّا ولا نصیرا»

3- Idols and tyrants (Taghuts) will be ineffective on the Day of Judgment. «لا یجدون ولیّا ولا نصیرا»

4- On the Day of Judgment, the disbelievers will gaze in every direction, but to what avail! «لایجدون...»

Ayah

يَوْمَ تُقَلَّبُ وُجُوهُهُمْ فِى ٱلنَّارِ يَقُولُونَ يَـٰلَيْتَنَآ أَطَعْنَا ٱللَّهَ وَأَطَعْنَا ٱلرَّسُولَا۠ (٦٦)
The day when their faces are turned about in the Fire, they will say, ‘We wish we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Apostle!’ (66)

Key Points

It is possible that the meaning of the Ayah is that the faces of the disbelievers will change into various colors due to the heat of the Fire.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the disbelievers will not only lack a guardian (wali) or helper (nasir), but they will also lack control over their own faces. «تقلّب وجوههم»

2- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical. «تقلّب وجوههم»

3- The heedless will awaken on the Day of Judgment. «یا لیتنا»

4- Regret will be of no benefit on the Day of Judgment. «یا لیتنا»

5- Obedience to Allah and the Messenger will be the wish of the inhabitants of Hellfire. «یا لیتنا اطعنا اللّه و اطعنا الرسولا»

6- Obedience to Allah, accompanied by obedience to the Messenger, is effective (or beneficial). «اطعنا اللّه و اطعنا الرسولا»

7- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, in addition to conveying the Divine command, also issued commands to the people himself. «اطعنا اللّه و اطعنا الرسولا»

8- The only path to salvation is obedience to Allah and the Messenger. «یا لیتنا اطعنا اللّه و اطعنا الرسولا»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ إِنَّآ أَطَعْنَا سَادَتَنَا وَكُبَرَآءَنَا فَأَضَلُّونَا ٱلسَّبِيلَا۠ (٦۷)
They will say, ‘Our Lord! We obeyed our leaders and elders, and they led us astray from the way.’ (67)

Key Points

Sādah (سادَة) is the plural of Sayyid (سیّد) and refers to the leaders and heads of a society. Kubarā’ (كُبَراء) is the plural of Kabīr (كبیر) and refers to the elders and senior members. Usually, these two groups play an important role in the customs, traditions, and behavior of the people.

Messages

1- Those who did not say "Rabbanā" (Our Lord) even once in this world will say "Rabbanā" in the Hereafter, accompanied by wailing and regret.

2- Criminals justify their mistakes and blame others. «انّا اطعنا سادتنا»

3- Blind imitation of fathers and the elders of society leads to regret. «انّا اطعنا سادتنا و كبرائنا فاضلّونا»

4- In the Hereafter, the disbelievers will confess to their deviation, but to no avail. «اضلّونا السّبیلا»

Ayahs

رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِهِمْ ضِعْفَيْنِ مِنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ وَٱلْعَنْهُمْ لَعْنًا كَبِيرًا (٦۸)
Our Lord! Give them a double punishment and curse them with a mighty curse.’ (68)
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِينَ ءَاذَوْا۟ مُوسَىٰ فَبَرَّأَهُ ٱللَّهُ مِمَّا قَالُوا۟ ۚ وَكَانَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَجِيهًا (٦۹)
O you who have faith! Do not be like those who offended Moses, whereat Allah cleared him of what they alleged, and he was distinguished in Allah’s sight. (69)

Key Points

In Ayah 57, the issue of harming the Prophet was raised. In this Ayah, [Allah] emphasizes that you should not be like the Children of Israel who harmed Moses. The meaning of the slander raised concerning Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) is either the accusation of a physical defect, or the accusation of murder, or sorcery and madness, the specific knowledge of which does not play a role in understanding the Ayah. The great curse is perhaps the same curse mentioned in Ayah 161 of Surah Al-Baqarah, where He says: «ان الّذین كفروا و ماتوا و هم كفّار اولئك علیهم لعنة اللّه و الملائكة و الناس اجمعین» Those who disbelieved and died while they were disbelievers—the curse of Allah and the angels and all people will be upon them.

Messages

1- The only prayer of the inhabitants of Hell that is answered is the prayer for punishment. (445) «ربّنا آتهم ضعفین من العذاب» because in another Ayah He says: «لكلّ ضعفٌ» (446)

2- Sometimes divine leaders are targeted by slander and harm, even from believers. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لا تكونوا»

3- The prerequisite of faith is avoiding slander. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لا تكونوا...»

4- Learn a lesson from the fact that slander against the friends of Allah (Awliya) remains ineffective, and do not slander others. «فبرّأه اللّه»

5- Allah clears His Prophets of the slanders made by enemies. «فبرّأه اللّه...»

6- Clearing the men of God from slander is not enough; they must also be honored. «فبرّأه اللّه ممّا قالوا و كان عند اللّه وجیها»

7- Being cleared of slander and possessing high standing (Wajahah) are conditions for success in leadership. «فبرّأه اللّه ممّا قالوا و كان عنداللّه وجیها»

8- Whoever you send on a mission and entrust with responsibility, you must support him. «فبرّأه اللّه ممّا قالوا و كان عنداللّه وجیها» (445) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

(446) Al-A'raf, 37.

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَقُولُوا۟ قَوْلًا سَدِيدًا (۷٠)
O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah, and speak upright words. (70)
يُصْلِحْ لَكُمْ أَعْمَـٰلَكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ ۗ وَمَن يُطِعِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ فَقَدْ فَازَ فَوْزًا عَظِيمًا (۷۱)
He will rectify your conduct for you and forgive you your sins. Whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle will certainly achieve a great success. (71)

Key Points

"Qawlan Sadeedan" is speech that contains no weakness, vanity, lies, or hypocrisy. It is speech that, like a firm dam, prevents doubt, suspicion, and corruption.

Messages

1- The prerequisite for faith is possessing piety (Taqwa), «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا اتّقوا اللّه», and the prerequisite for piety is sound speech. «اتّقوا اللّه و قولوا قولا سدیداً»

2- The believing person is worthy of piety and sound speech. «الّذین آمنوا - اتّقوا - قولوا»

3- The sign of faith is both external, «قولا سدیداً», and internal. «اتّقوا اللّه»

4- Sound speech is not only about its wording, but about its content. In reality, it is the thought and intellect that must be firm and sound. «قولوا قولاً سدیداً»

5- Not everything heard is suitable for repetition. «قولاً سدیداً»

6- If a person observes piety in their conduct and actions, Allah solves the remaining issues. «یصلح لكم اعمالكم و یغفر لكم» (Similar to Ayah 31 of Surah An-Nisa, where He says: «اِن تجتنبوا كبائر ما تُنهون عنه نكفّر عنكم سیّئاتكم», If you avoid the major sins which you are forbidden, We will cover your minor sins.)

7- The key to salvation is obedience to Allah and the Messenger. «و مَن یطع اللّه و رسوله فقد فاز فوزاً عظیماً»

Ayah

إِنَّا عَرَضْنَا ٱلْأَمَانَةَ عَلَى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱلْجِبَالِ فَأَبَيْنَ أَن يَحْمِلْنَهَا وَأَشْفَقْنَ مِنْهَا وَحَمَلَهَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ ظَلُومًا جَهُولًا (۷۲)
Indeed We presented the Trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to undertake it and were apprehensive of it; but man undertook it. Indeed he is most unjust and ignorant. (72)

Key Points

We read in a hadith: When the time for prayer arrived, a tremor would seize the body of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, and He would say: It is the time for prayer; prayer is that Divine Trust which the mountains and the heavens refused to bear. (447) In the Ziyarat Jami'ah Kabirah, addressing the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, we read: «انتم الامانة المحفوظة» (You are the preserved Trust).

There is a difference between bearing (haml) and imposition (tahmil). Man accepted the Divine Trust and bore it; it was not imposed upon him. Perhaps this Ayah expresses realities concerning man that human intellect has not yet grasped. However, what is understood from the apparent meaning of the Ayah is that Allah has granted mankind specific characteristics and privileges that none of the creatures in the heavens and the earth possess, and these privileges constitute the Divine Trust and bring responsibility upon man. But many humans betray this Trust and utilize it in ways contrary to the Will of Allah. Reason (aql) and free will (iradah), which should be employed in the path of recognizing the truth and choosing it to facilitate human growth and perfection, have been used in false ways, leading to the spread of oppression and tyranny, to the extent that human behavior is considered ignorant and foolish.

447) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Man is superior to the heavens and the earth. Man is Allah's trustee in existence. «انّا عرضنا الامانة على السموات و الارض... فابین ... و حملها الانسان»

2- Existence possesses consciousness. «عرضنا الامانة على السموات و الارض و... »

3- The Divine blessings granted to man (reason, free will, innate nature (fitrah), the guidance of the prophets, etc.) are a Divine Trust. Let us not betray the Trust, for that is among the greatest forms of oppression. «انّا عرضنا الامانة... ظلوماً»

4- What man accepted, but the heavens and the earth refused, was something other than devotion (qunut), glorification (tasbih), and prostration (sujud), because the heavens and the earth have readily accepted glorification and devotion. «و اِن من شى ء الا یسبّح بحمده» (448), «كلّ له قانتون» (449)

448) Isra, 44. 449) Rum, 26.

Ayah

لِّيُعَذِّبَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ وَٱلْمُنَـٰفِقَـٰتِ وَٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ وَٱلْمُشْرِكَـٰتِ وَيَتُوبَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًۢا (۷۳)
Allah will surely punish the hypocrites, men and women, and the polytheists, men and women, and Allah will turn clemently to the faithful, men and women, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (73)

Key Points

Allah's acceptance of repentance (Tawbah) upon His servants means granting them special favors, which is indicated by the particle 'alā (يتوب اللّه على). And the repentance of the servants is returning towards Allah and turning to Him, which is accompanied by ilā (إلى).

Messages

1- The Divine Trust brings responsibility. Whoever preserves the Trust is covered by mercy, and whoever betrays it through disbelief and polytheism will be punished. «ليعذّب اللّه المنافقين... و يتوب اللّه على المؤمنين»

2- The hypocrite is ranked alongside the polytheist. «المنافقين... المشركين»

3- Men and women are equal in ascending towards perfection or descending into degradation. «المنافقين و المنافقات، المشركين و المشركات، المؤمنين و المؤمنات»

4- Although Allah punishes the polytheists and hypocrites, the fundamental principle in His dealing with humans is forgiveness and mercy. «ليعذّب اللّه المنافقين... كان اللّه غفوراً رحيما»
